availableCommands.name: debug
availableCommands.description: Enable debug logging for this session and help diagnose issues (bundled)

availableCommands.name: simplify
availableCommands.description: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found. (bundled)

availableCommands.name: batch
availableCommands.description: Research and plan a large-scale change, then execute it in parallel across 5–30 isolated worktree agents that each open a PR. (bundled)

availableCommands.name: loop
availableCommands.description: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) (bundled)

availableCommands.name: claude-api
availableCommands.description:
Build apps with the Claude API or Anthropic SDK.
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`/`claude_agent_sdk`, or user asks to use Claude API, Anthropic SDKs, or Agent SDK.
DO NOT TRIGGER when: code imports `openai`/other AI SDK, general programming, or ML/data-science tasks. (bundled)

availableCommands.name: gsd:plan-phase
availableCommands.description: Create detailed phase plan (PLAN.md) with verification loop (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:settings
availableCommands.description: Configure GSD workflow toggles and model profile (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:add-todo
availableCommands.description: Capture idea or task as todo from current conversation context (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:audit-milestone
availableCommands.description: Audit milestone completion against original intent before archiving (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:insert-phase
availableCommands.description: Insert urgent work as decimal phase (e.g., 72.1) between existing phases (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:research-phase
availableCommands.description: Research how to implement a phase (standalone - usually use /gsd:plan-phase instead) (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:join-discord
availableCommands.description: Join the GSD Discord community (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:discuss-phase
availableCommands.description: Gather phase context through adaptive questioning before planning (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:verify-work
availableCommands.description: Validate built features through conversational UAT (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:remove-phase
availableCommands.description: Remove a future phase from roadmap and renumber subsequent phases (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:debug
availableCommands.description: Systematic debugging with persistent state across context resets (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:add-phase
availableCommands.description: Add phase to end of current milestone in roadmap (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:update
availableCommands.description: Update GSD to latest version with changelog display (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:quick
availableCommands.description: Execute a quick task with GSD guarantees (atomic commits, state tracking) but skip optional agents (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:help
availableCommands.description: Show available GSD commands and usage guide (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:cleanup
availableCommands.description: Archive accumulated phase directories from completed milestones (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:progress
availableCommands.description: Check project progress, show context, and route to next action (execute or plan) (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:health
availableCommands.description: Diagnose planning directory health and optionally repair issues (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:resume-work
availableCommands.description: Resume work from previous session with full context restoration (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:reapply-patches
availableCommands.description: Reapply local modifications after a GSD update (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:execute-phase
availableCommands.description: Execute all plans in a phase with wave-based parallelization (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:complete-milestone
availableCommands.description: Archive completed milestone and prepare for next version (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:new-project
availableCommands.description: Initialize a new project with deep context gathering and PROJECT.md (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:pause-work
availableCommands.description: Create context handoff when pausing work mid-phase (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:set-profile
availableCommands.description: Switch model profile for GSD agents (quality/balanced/budget) (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:check-todos
availableCommands.description: List pending todos and select one to work on (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:map-codebase
availableCommands.description: Analyze codebase with parallel mapper agents to produce .planning/codebase/ documents (user)

input.hint: [optional: specific area to map, e.g., 'api' or 'auth']

availableCommands.name: gsd:list-phase-assumptions
availableCommands.description: Surface Claude's assumptions about a phase approach before planning (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:plan-milestone-gaps
availableCommands.description: Create phases to close all gaps identified by milestone audit (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:new-milestone
availableCommands.description: Start a new milestone cycle — update PROJECT.md and route to requirements (user)

availableCommands.name: create-workflow
availableCommands.description: (kspec) Formalize repeatable patterns into trackable kspec workflows. Design steps, choose triggers, create loop variants, and integrate with skills.

availableCommands.name: help
availableCommands.description: (kspec) Get help with kspec commands and workflows

availableCommands.name: observe
availableCommands.description: (kspec) Capture and act on systemic patterns — friction, successes, questions, and ideas. The feedback loop that drives process improvement.

availableCommands.name: plan
availableCommands.description: (kspec) Translate approved plans into specs and tasks. Import structured documents or create incrementally. Plans persist as durable artifacts with audit trail.

availableCommands.name: reflect
availableCommands.description: (kspec) Structured reflection at the end of work sessions. Identifies learnings, friction points, and improvements for system evolution.

availableCommands.name: review
availableCommands.description: (kspec) Kspec-specific review gates — spec alignment, own AC coverage, trait AC coverage, and validation integration. Building block for project-specific review workflows.

availableCommands.name: task-work
availableCommands.description: (kspec) Structured task lifecycle — start, work, note, submit, complete. Fix cycle handling, scope management, loop mode for automation, and quality gates.

availableCommands.name: triage
availableCommands.description: (kspec) Triage inbox items systematically. Records decisions with audit trail, then executes actions. Supports inbox, observations, and automation eligibility triage.

availableCommands.name: triage-automation
availableCommands.description: (kspec) Assess and prepare tasks for automation eligibility. Verify spec coverage, acceptance criteria, and task readiness for automated agents.

availableCommands.name: triage-inbox
availableCommands.description: (kspec) Process inbox items using the record-act pattern. Categorize, promote to spec/task, merge duplicates, defer, or delete stale items with full audit trail.

availableCommands.name: writing-specs
availableCommands.description: (kspec) Create and maintain specification items — modules, features, requirements, acceptance criteria, and traits. The source of truth for what to build.

availableCommands.name: compact
availableCommands.description: Clear conversation history but keep a summary in context. Optional: /compact [instructions for summarization]

availableCommands.name: context
availableCommands.description: Show current context usage

availableCommands.name: heapdump
availableCommands.description: Dump the JS heap to ~/Desktop

availableCommands.name: init
availableCommands.description: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation

availableCommands.name: pr-comments
availableCommands.description: Get comments from a GitHub pull request

availableCommands.name: review
availableCommands.description: Review a pull request

availableCommands.name: security-review
availableCommands.description: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

availableCommands.name: extra-usage
availableCommands.description: Configure extra usage to keep working when limits are hit

availableCommands.name: insights
availableCommands.description: Generate a report analyzing your Claude Code sessions

toolResponse.stdout:
Bug audit: PTY lifecycle and backend switching
────────────────────────────────────────
ULID:      01KKAWKTJVBTXZDAF2293N8875
Slugs:     bug-pty
Type:      bug
Status:    pending
Priority:  2
Automation: eligible

─── Description ───
  Investigate and fix bugs in PTY session management. Check: PTY spawn error handling, resize debounce edge cases, output buffer overflow, backend switching race conditions, session ID preservation across switches, cleanup on unexpected PTY exit, enhanced PATH construction.
Spec ref:  @pty-lifecycle
Created:   2026-03-10T03:28:25.690Z

─── Spec Context ───
PTY Session Lifecycle
Type: feature
Implementation: implemented
Description:
  Spawn, manage, resize, and terminate PTY processes running AI CLI backends. Ring buffer captures last 200 lines with ANSI stripping.
Acceptance Criteria:
  [ac-1]
    Given: user opens a project
    When: a terminal session is spawned
    Then: a PTY process is created with node-pty using the resolved backend executable, correct cwd, and xterm-256color terminal type
  [ac-2]
    Given: a PTY session is running
    When: terminal output is produced
    Then: output is captured in a 200-line ring buffer with ANSI codes stripped and relayed to renderer via IPC
  [ac-3]
    Given: a PTY session is running
    When: the user closes the tab or app exits
    Then: the PTY process is terminated and all listeners are cleaned up

     1→import { BrowserWindow, ipcMain } from 'electron'
     2→import { PtyManager } from '../../pty-manager.js'
     3→import { SessionStore } from '../../session-store.js'
     4→import { ApiServerManager } from '../../api-server.js'
     5→import { pendingApiPrompts, autoCloseSessions } from '../api-prompt-handler.js'
     6→
     7→function maybeRespondToCursorPositionRequest(
     8→  ptyManager: PtyManager,
     9→  ptyToBackend: Map<string, string>,
    10→  ptyId: string,
    11→  data: string
    12→): void {
    13→  const backend = ptyToBackend.get(ptyId)
    14→  if (!backend || backend === 'claude') return
    15→  if (data.includes('\x1b[6n') || data.includes('\x1b[?6n')) {
    16→    ptyManager.write(ptyId, '\x1b[1;1R')
    17→  }
    18→}
    19→
    20→export function registerPtyHandlers(
    21→  ptyManager: PtyManager,
    22→  sessionStore: SessionStore,
    23→  apiServerManager: ApiServerManager,
    24→  ptyToProject: Map<string, string>,
    25→  ptyToBackend: Map<string, string>,
    26→  getMainWindow: () => BrowserWindow | null
    27→): void {
    28→  ipcMain.handle('pty:spawn', (_, { cwd, sessionId, model, backend }: { cwd: string; sessionId?: string; model?: string; backend?: 'default' | 'claude' | 'gemini' | 'codex' | 'opencode' | 'aider' }) => {
    29→    try {
    30→      const workspace = sessionStore.getWorkspace()
    31→      const project = workspace.projects.find(p => p.path === cwd)
    32→      const globalSettings = sessionStore.getSettings()
    33→
    34→      const normalizedGlobalBackend = globalSettings.backend === 'default'
    35→        ? undefined
    36→        : globalSettings.backend
    37→
    38→      const normalizedBackend = backend === 'default' ? undefined : backend
    39→      const effectiveBackend = normalizedBackend
    40→        || (project?.backend && project.backend !== 'default'
    41→          ? project.backend
    42→          : normalizedGlobalBackend || 'claude')
    43→
    44→      const pending = pendingApiPrompts.get(cwd)
    45→      const effectiveModel = model || pending?.model
    46→      const autoAcceptTools = project?.autoAcceptTools ?? globalSettings.autoAcceptTools
    47→      const permissionMode = project?.permissionMode ?? globalSettings.permissionMode
    48→
    49→      const id = ptyManager.spawn(cwd, sessionId, autoAcceptTools, permissionMode, effectiveModel, effectiveBackend)
    50→      ptyToProject.set(id, cwd)
    51→      ptyToBackend.set(id, effectiveBackend)
    52→
    53→      if (project?.apiPort && project.apiAutoStart && !apiServerManager.isRunning(cwd)) {
    54→        apiServerManager.start(cwd, project.apiPort)
    55→      }
    56→
    57→      const mainWindow = getMainWindow()
    58→
    59→      ptyManager.onData(id, (data) => {
    60→        maybeRespondToCursorPositionRequest(ptyManager, ptyToBackend, id, data)
    61→        try {
    62→          mainWindow?.webContents.send(`pty:data:${id}`, data)
    63→        } catch (e) {
    64→          console.error('IPC send failed', e)
    65→        }
    66→      })
    67→
    68→      ptyManager.onExit(id, (code) => {
    69→        try {
    70→          mainWindow?.webContents.send(`pty:exit:${id}`, code)
    71→        } catch (e) {
    72→          console.error('IPC send failed', e)
    73→        }
    74→        ptyToProject.delete(id)
    75→        ptyToBackend.delete(id)
    76→        autoCloseSessions.delete(id)
    77→      })
    78→
    79→      if (pending) {
    80→        pendingApiPrompts.delete(cwd)
    81→        if (pending.autoClose) autoCloseSessions.add(id)
    82→        setTimeout(() => {
    83→          ptyManager.write(id, pending.prompt + '\n')
    84→          pending.resolve({ success: true, message: 'Prompt sent to new terminal', sessionCreated: true })
    85→        }, 4000)
    86→      }
    87→
    88→      return id
    89→    } catch (error: any) {
    90→      console.error('Failed to spawn PTY:', error)
    91→      throw new Error(`Failed to start Claude: ${error.message}`)
    92→    }
    93→  })
    94→
    95→  ipcMain.on('pty:write', (_, { id, data }: { id: string; data: string }) => ptyManager.write(id, data))
    96→  ipcMain.on('pty:resize', (_, { id, cols, rows }: { id: string; cols: number; rows: number }) => ptyManager.resize(id, cols, rows))
    97→  ipcMain.on('pty:kill', (_, id: string) => {
    98→    ptyToProject.delete(id)
    99→    ptyToBackend.delete(id)
   100→    ptyManager.kill(id)
   101→  })
   102→
   103→  ipcMain.handle('pty:set-backend', async (_, { id: oldId, backend: newBackend }: { id: string; backend: 'claude' | 'gemini' | 'codex' | 'opencode' | 'aider' }) => {
   104→    const process = ptyManager.getProcess(oldId)
   105→    if (!process) return
   106→
   107→    const { cwd, sessionId, backend: oldBackend } = process
   108→    const effectiveSessionId = oldBackend !== newBackend ? undefined : sessionId
   109→
   110→    ptyManager.kill(oldId)
   111→    const newId = ptyManager.spawn(cwd, effectiveSessionId, undefined, undefined, undefined, newBackend)
   112→
   113→    const projectPath = ptyToProject.get(oldId)
   114→    if (projectPath) {
   115→      ptyToProject.set(newId, projectPath)
   116→      ptyToProject.delete(oldId)
   117→    }
   118→    ptyToBackend.set(newId, newBackend)
   119→    ptyToBackend.delete(oldId)
   120→
   121→    const mainWindow = getMainWindow()
   122→
   123→    ptyManager.onData(newId, (data) => {
   124→      maybeRespondToCursorPositionRequest(ptyManager, ptyToBackend, newId, data)
   125→      mainWindow?.webContents.send(`pty:data:${newId}`, data)
   126→    })
   127→
   128→    ptyManager.onExit(newId, (code) => {
   129→      mainWindow?.webContents.send(`pty:exit:${newId}`, code)
   130→      ptyToProject.delete(newId)
   131→      ptyToBackend.delete(newId)
   132→    })
   133→
   134→    mainWindow?.webContents.send('pty:recreated', { oldId, newId, backend: newBackend })
   135→  })
   136→}
   137→

     1→import { app } from 'electron'
     2→import { existsSync, mkdirSync, readFileSync, writeFileSync, renameSync, statSync } from 'fs'
     3→import { join } from 'path'
     4→import { syncMetaProjects } from './meta-project-sync'
     5→
     6→export interface ProjectCategory {
     7→  id: string
     8→  name: string
     9→  collapsed: boolean
    10→  order: number
    11→}
    12→
    13→export interface Project {
    14→  path: string
    15→  name: string
    16→  executable?: string
    17→  apiPort?: number  // Port for HTTP API to send prompts to terminal
    18→  apiAutoStart?: boolean  // Whether to auto-start API when session opens (default: false)
    19→  apiSessionMode?: 'existing' | 'new-keep' | 'new-close'  // How API requests handle sessions
    20→  apiModel?: 'default' | 'opus' | 'sonnet' | 'haiku'  // Model for API-triggered sessions
    21→  autoAcceptTools?: string[]  // Per-project tool patterns to auto-accept
    22→  permissionMode?: string     // Per-project permission mode
    23→  color?: string              // Project color for visual identification
    24→  ttsVoice?: string           // Per-project TTS voice (overrides global)
    25→  ttsEngine?: 'piper' | 'xtts'  // Per-project TTS engine
    26→  backend?: 'default' | 'claude' | 'gemini' | 'codex' | 'opencode' | 'aider' // Per-project backend (overrides global)
    27→  categoryId?: string         // Category this project belongs to
    28→  order?: number              // Order within category or uncategorized list
    29→}
    30→
    31→export interface OpenTab {
    32→  id: string
    33→  projectPath: string
    34→  sessionId?: string
    35→  title: string
    36→  ptyId?: string
    37→  backend?: 'default' | 'claude' | 'gemini' | 'codex' | 'opencode' | 'aider'
    38→}
    39→
    40→export interface TileLayout {
    41→  id: string
    42→  tabIds: string[]
    43→  activeTabId: string
    44→  x: number
    45→  y: number
    46→  width: number
    47→  height: number
    48→}
    49→
    50→export interface Workspace {
    51→  projects: Project[]
    52→  openTabs: OpenTab[]
    53→  activeTabId: string | null
    54→  viewMode?: 'tabs' | 'tiled'
    55→  tileLayout?: TileLayout[]
    56→  categories?: ProjectCategory[]
    57→}
    58→
    59→export interface WindowBounds {
    60→  x: number
    61→  y: number
    62→  width: number
    63→  height: number
    64→}
    65→
    66→export interface ThemeCustomization {
    67→  accentColor: string | null
    68→  backgroundColor: string | null
    69→  textColor: string | null
    70→  terminalColors: {
    71→    black?: string
    72→    red?: string
    73→    green?: string
    74→    yellow?: string
    75→    blue?: string
    76→    magenta?: string
    77→    cyan?: string
    78→    white?: string
    79→  } | null
    80→}
    81→
    82→export interface Settings {
    83→  defaultProjectDir: string
    84→  theme: string
    85→  themeCustomization?: ThemeCustomization | null
    86→  voiceOutputEnabled?: boolean
    87→  voiceVolume?: number
    88→  voiceSpeed?: number
    89→  voiceSkipOnNew?: boolean
    90→  voiceSilenceThreshold?: number
    91→  autoAcceptTools?: string[]
    92→  permissionMode?: string
    93→  backend?: 'default' | 'claude' | 'gemini' | 'codex' | 'opencode' | 'aider'
    94→}
    95→
    96→
    97→interface StoredData {
    98→  workspace: Workspace
    99→  windowBounds?: WindowBounds
   100→  settings?: Settings
   101→}
   102→
   103→export class SessionStore {
   104→  private configPath: string
   105→  private backupPath: string
   106→  private data: StoredData
   107→
   108→  constructor() {
   109→    const configDir = join(app.getPath('userData'), 'config')
   110→    if (!existsSync(configDir)) {
   111→      mkdirSync(configDir, { recursive: true })
   112→    }
   113→    this.configPath = join(configDir, 'workspace.json')
   114→    this.backupPath = join(configDir, 'workspace.json.backup')
   115→    this.data = this.load()
   116→  }
   117→
   118→  private loadFile(path: string): StoredData | null {
   119→    try {
   120→      if (!existsSync(path)) return null
   121→      const stat = statSync(path)
   122→      if (stat.size === 0) return null
   123→      const content = readFileSync(path, 'utf-8')
   124→      const data = JSON.parse(content)
   125→      if (!data?.workspace) return null
   126→      return data
   127→    } catch (e) {
   128→      console.error(`[SessionStore] Failed to load ${path}:`, e)
   129→      return null
   130→    }
   131→  }
   132→
   133→  private load(): StoredData {
   134→    // Try main file first
   135→    const main = this.loadFile(this.configPath)
   136→    if (main && (main.workspace.projects?.length || 0) > 0) {
   137→      return main
   138→    }
   139→
   140→    // Main file is empty/corrupt/missing — try backup
   141→    const backup = this.loadFile(this.backupPath)
   142→    if (backup && (backup.workspace.projects?.length || 0) > 0) {
   143→      console.log('[SessionStore] Main config empty/corrupt, restored from backup (' +
   144→        backup.workspace.projects.length + ' projects)')
   145→      // Restore backup as main file immediately
   146→      try {
   147→        writeFileSync(this.configPath, JSON.stringify(backup, null, 2))
   148→      } catch (e) {
   149→        console.error('[SessionStore] Failed to restore backup to main:', e)
   150→      }
   151→      return backup
   152→    }
   153→
   154→    // If main loaded but had 0 projects (valid empty state), use it
   155→    if (main) return main
   156→
   157→    return {
   158→      workspace: {
   159→        projects: [],
   160→        openTabs: [],
   161→        activeTabId: null
   162→      }
   163→    }
   164→  }
   165→
   166→  private save(): void {
   167→    try {
   168→      const json = JSON.stringify(this.data, null, 2)
   169→
   170→      // Validate we can parse what we're about to write
   171→      JSON.parse(json)
   172→
   173→      // Back up existing file before overwriting (if it has content)
   174→      if (existsSync(this.configPath)) {
   175→        try {
   176→          const stat = statSync(this.configPath)
   177→          if (stat.size > 0) {
   178→            // Only update backup if existing file is valid and has projects
   179→            const existing = this.loadFile(this.configPath)
   180→            if (existing && (existing.workspace.projects?.length || 0) > 0) {
   181→              writeFileSync(this.backupPath, readFileSync(this.configPath))
   182→            }
   183→          }
   184→        } catch (e) {
   185→          console.error('[SessionStore] Failed to update backup:', e)
   186→        }
   187→      }
   188→
   189→      // Atomic write: write to temp file, then rename
   190→      const tmpPath = this.configPath + '.tmp'
   191→      writeFileSync(tmpPath, json)
   192→
   193→      // Verify the temp file was written completely
   194→      const written = readFileSync(tmpPath, 'utf-8')
   195→      JSON.parse(written) // throws if truncated/corrupt
   196→
   197→      renameSync(tmpPath, this.configPath)
   198→    } catch (e) {
   199→      console.error('[SessionStore] Failed to save workspace:', e)
   200→    }
   201→  }
   202→
   203→  getWorkspace(): Workspace {
   204→    return this.data.workspace
   205→  }
   206→
   207→  // Reload workspace from disk (useful when file was modified externally)
   208→  reloadFromDisk(): void {
   209→    console.log('[SessionStore] Reloading workspace from disk')
   210→    this.data = this.load()
   211→    console.log('[SessionStore] Reloaded, projects:', this.data.workspace?.projects?.length || 0)
   212→  }
   213→
   214→  saveWorkspace(workspace: Workspace): void {
   215→    // Protect against overwriting populated workspace with empty one
   216→    const incomingProjects = workspace?.projects?.length || 0
   217→    const currentProjects = this.data.workspace?.projects?.length || 0
   218→    if (incomingProjects === 0 && currentProjects > 0) {
   219→      console.log('[SessionStore] Rejected empty workspace save - current has', currentProjects, 'projects')
   220→      return
   221→    }
   222→    this.data.workspace = workspace
   223→    this.save()
   224→    syncMetaProjects(workspace)
   225→  }
   226→
   227→  getWindowBounds(): WindowBounds | undefined {
   228→    return this.data.windowBounds
   229→  }
   230→
   231→  saveWindowBounds(bounds: WindowBounds): void {
   232→    this.data.windowBounds = bounds
   233→    this.save()
   234→  }
   235→
   236→  getSettings(): Settings {
   237→    return this.data.settings ?? { defaultProjectDir: '', theme: 'default', backend: 'default' }
   238→  }
   239→
   240→  saveSettings(settings: Settings): void {
   241→    this.data.settings = settings
   242→    this.save()
   243→  }
   244→}
   245→
```

1→/**
     2→ * PTY Routes - /api/pty/* endpoints
     3→ */
     4→
     5→import { Express, Request, Response } from 'express'
     6→import { WebSocket } from 'ws'
     7→import { validateProjectPath } from '../../mobile-security'
     8→import { log } from '../utils'
     9→import { LocalPty } from '../types'
    10→
    11→export function setupPtyRoutes(
    12→  app: Express,
    13→  getPtyManager: () => any,
    14→  getLocalPtys: () => Map<string, LocalPty>,
    15→  getPtyStreams: () => Map<string, Set<WebSocket>>,
    16→  getPtyDataBuffer: () => Map<string, string[]>,
    17→  broadcastPtyData: (ptyId: string, data: string) => void,
    18→  broadcastPtyExit: (ptyId: string, code: number) => void
    19→): void {
    20→  // Spawn a new PTY
    21→  app.post('/api/pty/spawn', async (req: Request, res: Response) => {
    22→    try {
    23→      const { projectPath, sessionId, model, backend } = req.body
    24→
    25→      if (!projectPath || typeof projectPath !== 'string') {
    26→        return res.status(400).json({ error: 'projectPath is required' })
    27→      }
    28→
    29→      const pathValidation = validateProjectPath(projectPath)
    30→      if (!pathValidation.valid) {
    31→        return res.status(400).json({ error: pathValidation.error })
    32→      }
    33→      const safeProjectPath = pathValidation.normalizedPath!
    34→
    35→      const ptyManager = getPtyManager()
    36→      if (!ptyManager) {
    37→        return res.status(500).json({ error: 'PTY manager not available' })
    38→      }
    39→
    40→      log('PTY spawn request', { projectPath: safeProjectPath, sessionId, model, backend })
    41→
    42→      const ptyId = ptyManager.spawn(
    43→        safeProjectPath,
    44→        sessionId,
    45→        undefined, // autoAcceptTools
    46→        undefined, // permissionMode
    47→        model,
    48→        backend
    49→      )
    50→
    51→      // Track this PTY for cleanup
    52→      const localPty: LocalPty = {
    53→        ptyId,
    54→        projectPath: safeProjectPath,
    55→        dataCallbacks: new Set(),
    56→        exitCallbacks: new Set()
    57→      }
    58→      getLocalPtys().set(ptyId, localPty)
    59→
    60→      // Set up data forwarding to WebSocket streams
    61→      ptyManager.onData(ptyId, (data: string) => {
    62→        broadcastPtyData(ptyId, data)
    63→      })
    64→
    65→      // Set up exit handler
    66→      ptyManager.onExit(ptyId, (code: number) => {
    67→        log('PTY exited', { ptyId, code })
    68→        broadcastPtyExit(ptyId, code)
    69→        getLocalPtys().delete(ptyId)
    70→        getPtyStreams().delete(ptyId)
    71→        getPtyDataBuffer().delete(ptyId)
    72→      })
    73→
    74→      log('PTY spawned', { ptyId, projectPath })
    75→      res.json({ ptyId })
    76→    } catch (error) {
    77→      log('PTY spawn error', { error: String(error) })
    78→      res.status(500).json({ error: String(error) })
    79→    }
    80→  })
    81→
    82→  // Write data to PTY
    83→  app.post('/api/pty/:id/write', (req: Request, res: Response) => {
    84→    try {
    85→      const { id } = req.params
    86→      const { data } = req.body
    87→
    88→      if (!data || typeof data !== 'string') {
    89→        return res.status(400).json({ error: 'data is required and must be a string' })
    90→      }
    91→
    92→      const ptyManager = getPtyManager()
    93→      if (!ptyManager) {
    94→        return res.status(500).json({ error: 'PTY manager not available' })
    95→      }
    96→
    97→      if (!ptyManager.getProcess(id)) {
    98→        return res.status(404).json({ error: 'PTY not found' })
    99→      }
   100→
   101→      ptyManager.write(id, data)

     1→/**
     2→ * PTY Routes - /api/pty/* endpoints
     3→ */
     4→
     5→import { Express, Request, Response } from 'express'
     6→import { WebSocket } from 'ws'
     7→import { validateProjectPath } from '../../mobile-security'
     8→import { log } from '../utils'
     9→import { LocalPty } from '../types'
    10→
    11→export function setupPtyRoutes(
    12→  app: Express,
    13→  getPtyManager: () => any,
    14→  getLocalPtys: () => Map<string, LocalPty>,
    15→  getPtyStreams: () => Map<string, Set<WebSocket>>,
    16→  getPtyDataBuffer: () => Map<string, string[]>,
    17→  broadcastPtyData: (ptyId: string, data: string) => void,
    18→  broadcastPtyExit: (ptyId: string, code: number) => void
    19→): void {
    20→  // Spawn a new PTY
    21→  app.post('/api/pty/spawn', async (req: Request, res: Response) => {
    22→    try {
    23→      const { projectPath, sessionId, model, backend } = req.body
    24→
    25→      if (!projectPath || typeof projectPath !== 'string') {
    26→        return res.status(400).json({ error: 'projectPath is required' })
    27→      }
    28→
    29→      const pathValidation = validateProjectPath(projectPath)
    30→      if (!pathValidation.valid) {
    31→        return res.status(400).json({ error: pathValidation.error })
    32→      }
    33→      const safeProjectPath = pathValidation.normalizedPath!
    34→
    35→      const ptyManager = getPtyManager()
    36→      if (!ptyManager) {
    37→        return res.status(500).json({ error: 'PTY manager not available' })
    38→      }
    39→
    40→      log('PTY spawn request', { projectPath: safeProjectPath, sessionId, model, backend })
    41→
    42→      const ptyId = ptyManager.spawn(
    43→        safeProjectPath,
    44→        sessionId,
    45→        undefined, // autoAcceptTools
    46→        undefined, // permissionMode
    47→        model,
    48→        backend
    49→      )
    50→
    51→      // Track this PTY for cleanup
    52→      const localPty: LocalPty = {
    53→        ptyId,
    54→        projectPath: safeProjectPath,
    55→        dataCallbacks: new Set(),
    56→        exitCallbacks: new Set()
    57→      }
    58→      getLocalPtys().set(ptyId, localPty)
    59→
    60→      // Set up data forwarding to WebSocket streams
    61→      ptyManager.onData(ptyId, (data: string) => {
    62→        broadcastPtyData(ptyId, data)
    63→      })
    64→
    65→      // Set up exit handler
    66→      ptyManager.onExit(ptyId, (code: number) => {
    67→        log('PTY exited', { ptyId, code })
    68→        broadcastPtyExit(ptyId, code)
    69→        getLocalPtys().delete(ptyId)
    70→        getPtyStreams().delete(ptyId)
    71→        getPtyDataBuffer().delete(ptyId)
    72→      })
    73→
    74→      log('PTY spawned', { ptyId, projectPath })
    75→      res.json({ ptyId })
    76→    } catch (error) {
    77→      log('PTY spawn error', { error: String(error) })
    78→      res.status(500).json({ error: String(error) })
    79→    }
    80→  })
    81→
    82→  // Write data to PTY
    83→  app.post('/api/pty/:id/write', (req: Request, res: Response) => {
    84→    try {
    85→      const { id } = req.params
    86→      const { data } = req.body
    87→
    88→      if (!data || typeof data !== 'string') {
    89→        return res.status(400).json({ error: 'data is required and must be a string' })
    90→      }
    91→
    92→      const ptyManager = getPtyManager()
    93→      if (!ptyManager) {
    94→        return res.status(500).json({ error: 'PTY manager not available' })
    95→      }
    96→
    97→      if (!ptyManager.getProcess(id)) {
    98→        return res.status(404).json({ error: 'PTY not found' })
    99→      }
   100→
   101→      ptyManager.write(id, data)
   102→      log('PTY write', { ptyId: id, dataLength: data.length })
   103→      res.json({ success: true })
   104→    } catch (error) {
   105→      log('PTY write error', { error: String(error) })
   106→      res.status(500).json({ error: String(error) })
   107→    }
   108→  })
   109→
   110→  // Resize PTY
   111→  app.post('/api/pty/:id/resize', (req: Request, res: Response) => {
   112→    try {
   113→      const { id } = req.params
   114→      const { cols, rows } = req.body
   115→
   116→      if (typeof cols !== 'number' || typeof rows !== 'number') {
   117→        return res.status(400).json({ error: 'cols and rows are required and must be numbers' })
   118→      }
   119→
   120→      if (cols < 1 || rows < 1 || cols > 500 || rows > 500) {
   121→        return res.status(400).json({ error: 'cols and rows must be between 1 and 500' })
   122→      }
   123→
   124→      const ptyManager = getPtyManager()
   125→      if (!ptyManager) {
   126→        return res.status(500).json({ error: 'PTY manager not available' })
   127→      }
   128→
   129→      if (!ptyManager.getProcess(id)) {
   130→        return res.status(404).json({ error: 'PTY not found' })
   131→      }
   132→
   133→      ptyManager.resize(id, cols, rows)
   134→      log('PTY resize', { ptyId: id, cols, rows })
   135→      res.json({ success: true })
   136→    } catch (error) {
   137→      log('PTY resize error', { error: String(error) })
   138→      res.status(500).json({ error: String(error) })
   139→    }
   140→  })
   141→
   142→  // Kill PTY
   143→  app.delete('/api/pty/:id', (req: Request, res: Response) => {
   144→    try {
   145→      const { id } = req.params
   146→
   147→      const ptyManager = getPtyManager()
   148→      if (!ptyManager) {
   149→        return res.status(500).json({ error: 'PTY manager not available' })
   150→      }
   151→
   152→      if (!ptyManager.getProcess(id)) {
   153→        return res.status(404).json({ error: 'PTY not found' })
   154→      }
   155→
   156→      ptyManager.kill(id)
   157→      getLocalPtys().delete(id)
   158→
   159→      // Close any WebSocket streams for this PTY
   160→      const streams = getPtyStreams().get(id)
   161→      if (streams) {
   162→        streams.forEach(ws => {
   163→          if (ws.readyState === WebSocket.OPEN) {
   164→            ws.close(1000, 'PTY killed')
   165→          }
   166→        })
   167→        getPtyStreams().delete(id)
   168→      }
   169→
   170→      log('PTY killed', { ptyId: id })
   171→      res.json({ success: true })
   172→    } catch (error) {
   173→      log('PTY kill error', { error: String(error) })
   174→      res.status(500).json({ error: String(error) })
   175→    }
   176→  })
   177→}

1→/**
     2→ * PTY WebSocket Management
     3→ *
     4→ * Manages WebSocket connections for PTY data streaming.
     5→ */
     6→
     7→import { MAX_RECONNECT_ATTEMPTS, RECONNECT_DELAYS } from './constants'
     8→import { PtyWebSocketState } from './types'
     9→
    10→export class PtyWebSocketManager {
    11→  private ptyWebsockets: Map<string, PtyWebSocketState> = new Map()
    12→  private wsBaseUrl: string
    13→  private token: string
    14→
    15→  constructor(wsBaseUrl: string, token: string) {
    16→    this.wsBaseUrl = wsBaseUrl
    17→    this.token = token
    18→  }
    19→
    20→  getPtyWebsockets(): Map<string, PtyWebSocketState> {
    21→    return this.ptyWebsockets
    22→  }
    23→
    24→  /**
    25→   * Connect WebSocket for PTY data streaming
    26→   */
    27→  connectPtyStream(ptyId: string): void {
    28→    // Don't reconnect if already connected or connecting
    29→    const existing = this.ptyWebsockets.get(ptyId)
    30→    if (
    31→      existing?.ws &&
    32→      (existing.ws.readyState === WebSocket.OPEN || existing.ws.readyState === WebSocket.CONNECTING)
    33→    ) {
    34→      return
    35→    }
    36→
    37→    const url = `${this.wsBaseUrl}/api/pty/${ptyId}/stream?token=${encodeURIComponent(this.token)}`
    38→    console.log('[HttpBackend] Connecting PTY stream:', ptyId)
    39→
    40→    const ws = new WebSocket(url)
    41→
    42→    // Reuse existing state if available (preserves callbacks), otherwise create new
    43→    const state: PtyWebSocketState = existing || {
    44→      ws,
    45→      dataCallbacks: new Set(),
    46→      exitCallbacks: new Set(),
    47→      reconnectAttempts: 0,
    48→      reconnectTimer: null,
    49→      dataBuffer: []
    50→    }
    51→    // Update the WebSocket reference
    52→    state.ws = ws
    53→    state.reconnectAttempts = existing?.reconnectAttempts || 0
    54→
    55→    ws.onopen = () => {
    56→      console.log('[HttpBackend] PTY stream connected:', ptyId)
    57→      state.reconnectAttempts = 0
    58→    }
    59→
    60→    ws.onmessage = (event) => {
    61→      try {
    62→        const msg = JSON.parse(event.data)
    63→
    64→        switch (msg.type) {
    65→          case 'data':
    66→            // Buffer data if no callbacks registered yet
    67→            if (state.dataCallbacks.size === 0) {
    68→              state.dataBuffer.push(msg.data)
    69→              // Limit buffer size to prevent memory issues
    70→              if (state.dataBuffer.length > 1000) {
    71→                state.dataBuffer.shift()
    72→              }
    73→            } else {
    74→              // Forward data to all callbacks
    75→              state.dataCallbacks.forEach((cb) => cb(msg.data))
    76→            }
    77→            break
    78→
    79→          case 'exit':
    80→            // Forward exit to all callbacks
    81→            state.exitCallbacks.forEach((cb) => cb(msg.code))
    82→            // Clean up after exit
    83→            this.ptyWebsockets.delete(ptyId)
    84→            break
    85→
    86→          case 'connected':
    87→            console.log(
    88→              '[HttpBackend] PTY stream confirmed:',
    89→              ptyId,
    90→              'callbacks:',
    91→              state.dataCallbacks.size,
    92→              'buffered:',
    93→              state.dataBuffer.length
    94→            )
    95→            break
    96→
    97→          case 'pong':
    98→            // Keep-alive response, nothing to do
    99→            break
   100→
   101→          default:
   102→            console.log('[HttpBackend] Unknown PTY message type:', msg.type)
   103→        }
   104→      } catch (e) {
   105→        console.error('[HttpBackend] Failed to parse PTY message:', e)
   106→      }
   107→    }
   108→
   109→    ws.onerror = (event) => {
   110→      console.error('[HttpBackend] PTY stream error:', ptyId, event)
   111→    }
   112→
   113→    ws.onclose = (event) => {
   114→      console.log('[HttpBackend] PTY stream closed:', ptyId, event.code, event.reason)
   115→
   116→      // Attempt reconnection if not a clean close and we still want this PTY
   117→      if (!event.wasClean && state.reconnectAttempts < MAX_RECONNECT_ATTEMPTS) {
   118→        const delay =
   119→          RECONNECT_DELAYS[Math.min(state.reconnectAttempts, RECONNECT_DELAYS.length - 1)]
   120→        console.log(`[HttpBackend] Reconnecting PTY stream in ${delay}ms...`)
   121→
   122→        state.reconnectTimer = setTimeout(() => {
   123→          state.reconnectAttempts++
   124→          this.connectPtyStream(ptyId)
   125→        }, delay)
   126→      } else if (event.code !== 1000) {
   127→        // Exit callback for abnormal closure
   128→        state.exitCallbacks.forEach((cb) => cb(-1))
   129→        this.ptyWebsockets.delete(ptyId)
   130→      }
   131→    }
   132→
   133→    this.ptyWebsockets.set(ptyId, state)
   134→  }
   135→
   136→  /**
   137→   * Disconnect PTY WebSocket stream
   138→   */
   139→  disconnectPtyStream(ptyId: string): void {
   140→    const state = this.ptyWebsockets.get(ptyId)
   141→    if (!state) return
   142→
   143→    // Clear reconnect timer
   144→    if (state.reconnectTimer) {
   145→      clearTimeout(state.reconnectTimer)
   146→    }
   147→
   148→    // Close WebSocket
   149→    if (
   150→      state.ws &&
   151→      (state.ws.readyState === WebSocket.OPEN || state.ws.readyState === WebSocket.CONNECTING)
   152→    ) {
   153→      state.ws.close(1000, 'Client requested close')
   154→    }
   155→
   156→    this.ptyWebsockets.delete(ptyId)
   157→  }
   158→
   159→  /**
   160→   * Disconnect all PTY WebSocket connections
   161→   */
   162→  disconnectAll(): void {
   163→    this.ptyWebsockets.forEach((state, ptyId) => {
   164→      if (state.reconnectTimer) {
   165→        clearTimeout(state.reconnectTimer)
   166→      }
   167→      if (
   168→        state.ws &&
   169→        (state.ws.readyState === WebSocket.OPEN || state.ws.readyState === WebSocket.CONNECTING)
   170→      ) {
   171→        state.ws.close(1000, 'Client disconnecting')
   172→      }
   173→    })
   174→    this.ptyWebsockets.clear()
   175→  }
   176→
   177→  /**
   178→   * Send data to a PTY via WebSocket
   179→   */
   180→  sendToPty(ptyId: string, message: object): boolean {
   181→    const state = this.ptyWebsockets.get(ptyId)
   182→    if (state && state.ws.readyState === WebSocket.OPEN) {
   183→      state.ws.send(JSON.stringify(message))
   184→      return true
   185→    }
   186→    return false
   187→  }
   188→}
   189→

   174→    this.ptyWebsockets.clear()
   175→  }
   176→
   177→  /**
   178→   * Send data to a PTY via WebSocket
   179→   */
   180→  sendToPty(ptyId: string, message: object): boolean {
   181→    const state = this.ptyWebsockets.get(ptyId)
   182→    if (state && state.ws.readyState === WebSocket.OPEN) {
   183→      state.ws.send(JSON.stringify(message))
   184→      return true
   185→    }
   186→    return false
   187→  }
   188→}

    10→export function getDefaultShell(): string {

1→/**
     2→ * Electron Backend Implementation
     3→ *
     4→ * This module provides the Electron IPC implementation of the Api interface.
     5→ * It delegates all calls to window.electronAPI which communicates with the
     6→ * Electron main process via IPC.
     7→ */
     8→
     9→import {
    10→  Api,
    11→  ExtendedApi,
    12→  Settings,
    13→  Workspace,
    14→  Session,
    15→  PtyDataCallback,
    16→  PtyExitCallback,
    17→  PtyRecreatedCallback,
    18→  ApiOpenSessionCallback,
    19→  Unsubscribe
    20→} from './types'
    21→import type { BackendId } from './types'
    22→
    23→/**
    24→ * Type declaration for the global electronAPI
    25→ */
    26→declare global {
    27→  interface Window {
    28→    electronAPI?: {
    29→      // PTY Management
    30→      spawnPty: (cwd: string, sessionId?: string, model?: string, backend?: BackendId) => Promise<string>
    31→      killPty: (id: string) => void
    32→      writePty: (id: string, data: string) => void
    33→      resizePty: (id: string, cols: number, rows: number) => void
    34→      onPtyData: (id: string, callback: (data: string) => void) => () => void
    35→      onPtyExit: (id: string, callback: (code: number) => void) => () => void
    36→      onPtyRecreated: (callback: (data: { oldId: string; newId: string; backend: BackendId }) => void) => () => void
    37→      setPtyBackend: (id: string, backend: BackendId) => Promise<void>
    38→
    39→
    40→      // Session Management
    41→      discoverSessions: (projectPath: string, backend?: BackendId) => Promise<Session[]>
    42→
    43→      // Workspace Management
    44→      getWorkspace: () => Promise<Workspace>
    45→      saveWorkspace: (workspace: Workspace) => Promise<void>
    46→
    47→      // Settings Management
    48→      getSettings: () => Promise<Settings>
    49→      saveSettings: (settings: Settings) => Promise<void>
    50→
    51→      // Project Management

    53→      addProjectsFromParent: () => Promise<Array<{ path: string; name: string }> | null>

    55→      // TTS

   108→      // Kspec

   118→

   121→      onInstallProgress: (callback: (data: { type: string; status: string; percent?: number }) => void) => () => void
   122→    }
   123→  }
   124→}
   125→
   126→/**
   127→ * Electron backend implementation that delegates to window.electronAPI
   128→ */
   129→export class ElectronBackend implements ExtendedApi {
   130→  /**
   131→   * Check if the Electron API is available
   132→   * @throws Error if electronAPI is not available
   133→   */
   134→  private checkApi(): void {
   135→    if (!window.electronAPI) {
   136→      throw new Error('Electron API not available. Are you running in Electron?')
   137→    }
   138→  }
   139→
   140→  // ==========================================================================
   141→  // PTY Management
   142→  // ==========================================================================
   143→
   144→  async spawnPty(cwd: string, sessionId?: string, model?: string, backend?: BackendId): Promise<string> {
   145→    this.checkApi()
   146→    return window.electronAPI!.spawnPty(cwd, sessionId, model, backend)
   147→  }
   148→
   149→  killPty(id: string): void {
   150→    this.checkApi()
   151→    window.electronAPI!.killPty(id)
   152→  }
   153→
   154→  writePty(id: string, data: string): void {
   155→    this.checkApi()
   156→    window.electronAPI!.writePty(id, data)
   157→  }
   158→
   159→  resizePty(id: string, cols: number, rows: number): void {
   160→    this.checkApi()
   161→    window.electronAPI!.resizePty(id, cols, rows)
   162→  }
   163→
   164→  onPtyData(id: string, callback: PtyDataCallback): Unsubscribe {
   165→    this.checkApi()
   166→    return window.electronAPI!.onPtyData(id, callback)
   167→  }
   168→
   169→  onPtyExit(id: string, callback: PtyExitCallback): Unsubscribe {
   170→    this.checkApi()
   171→    return window.electronAPI!.onPtyExit(id, callback)
   172→  }
   173→
   174→  onPtyRecreated(callback: PtyRecreatedCallback): Unsubscribe {
   175→    this.checkApi()
   176→    return window.electronAPI!.onPtyRecreated(callback)
   177→  }
   178→
   179→  setPtyBackend(id: string, backend: BackendId): Promise<void> {
   180→    this.checkApi()
   181→    return window.electronAPI!.setPtyBackend(id, backend)
   182→  }
   183→
   184→  // ==========================================================================
   185→  // Session Management
   186→  // ==========================================================================
   187→
   188→  async discoverSessions(projectPath: string, backend?: BackendId): Promise<Session[]> {
   189→    this.checkApi()
   190→    return window.electronAPI!.discoverSessions(projectPath, backend)
   191→  }
   192→
   193→  // ==========================================================================
   194→  // Workspace Management
   195→  // ==========================================================================
   196→
   197→  async getWorkspace(): Promise<Workspace> {
   198→    this.checkApi()
   199→    return window.electronAPI!.getWorkspace()
   200→  }
   201→
   202→  async saveWorkspace(workspace: Workspace): Promise<void> {
   203→    this.checkApi()
   204→    return window.electronAPI!.saveWorkspace(workspace)
   205→  }
   206→
   207→  // ==========================================================================
   208→  // Settings Management
   209→  // ==========================================================================
   210→
   211→  async getSettings(): Promise<Settings> {
   212→    this.checkApi()
   213→    return window.electronAPI!.getSettings()
   214→  }
   215→
   216→  async saveSettings(settings: Settings): Promise<void> {
   217→    this.checkApi()
   218→    return window.electronAPI!.saveSettings(settings)
   219→  }
   220→
   221→  // ==========================================================================
   222→  // Project Management
   223→  // ==========================================================================
   224→
   225→  async addProject(): Promise<string | null> {
   226→    this.checkApi()
   227→    return window.electronAPI!.addProject()
   228→  }
   229→
   230→  async addProjectsFromParent(): Promise<Array<{ path: string; name: string }> | null> {
   231→    this.checkApi()
   232→    return window.electronAPI!.addProjectsFromParent()
   233→  }
   234→
   235→  // ==========================================================================
   236→  // TTS (Text-to-Speech)
   237→  // ==========================================================================
   238→
   239→  async ttsInstallInstructions(projectPath: string): Promise<{ success: boolean }> {
   240→    this.checkApi()
   241→    return window.electronAPI!.ttsInstallInstructions(projectPath)
   242→  }
   243→
   244→  async ttsSpeak(text: string): Promise<{ success: boolean; audioData?: string; error?: string }> {
   245→    this.checkApi()
   246→    return window.electronAPI!.voiceSpeak(text)
   247→  }
   248→
   249→  async ttsStop(): Promise<{ success: boolean }> {
   250→    this.checkApi()
   251→    return window.electronAPI!.voiceStopSpeaking()
   252→  }
   253→
   254→  // ==========================================================================
   255→  // Events
   256→  // ==========================================================================
   257→
   258→  onApiOpenSession(callback: ApiOpenSessionCallback): Unsubscribe {
   259→    this.checkApi()
   260→    return window.electronAPI!.onApiOpenSession(callback)
   261→  }
   262→
   263→  // ==========================================================================
   264→  // Extended API (Desktop-only features)
   265→  // ==========================================================================
   266→
   267→  async selectDirectory(): Promise<string | null> {
   268→    this.checkApi()
   269→    return window.electronAPI!.selectDirectory()
   270→  }
   271→
   272→  async selectExecutable(): Promise<string | null> {
   273→    this.checkApi()
   274→    return window.electronAPI!.selectExecutable()
   275→  }
   276→
   277→  windowMinimize(): void {
   278→    this.checkApi()
   279→    window.electronAPI!.windowMinimize()
   280→  }
   281→
   282→  windowMaximize(): void {
   283→    this.checkApi()
   284→    window.electronAPI!.windowMaximize()
   285→  }
   286→
   287→  windowClose(): void {
   288→    this.checkApi()
   289→    window.electronAPI!.windowClose()
   290→  }
   291→
   292→  async windowIsMaximized(): Promise<boolean> {
   293→    this.checkApi()
   294→    return window.electronAPI!.windowIsMaximized()
   295→  }
   296→
   297→  getPathForFile(file: File): string {
   298→    this.checkApi()
   299→    return window.electronAPI!.getPathForFile(file)
   300→  }
   301→
   302→  async readClipboardImage(): Promise<{ success: boolean; hasImage?: boolean; path?: string; error?: string }> {
   303→    this.checkApi()
   304→    return window.electronAPI!.readClipboardImage()
   305→  }
   306→

   314→    return window.electronAPI!.isDebugMode()
   315→  }
   316→
   317→  async refresh(): Promise<void> {
   318→    this.checkApi()
   319→    return window.electronAPI!.refresh()
   320→  }
   321→
   322→  async openExternal(url: string): Promise<void> {
   323→    this.checkApi()
   324→    return window.electronAPI!.openExternal(url)
   325→  }
   326→
   327→  debugLog(message: string): void {
   328→    this.checkApi()
   329→    window.electronAPI!.debugLog(message)
   330→  }
   331→}
   332→
   333→/**
   334→ * Singleton instance of the Electron backend
   335→ */
   336→let instance: ElectronBackend | null = null
   337→
   338→/**
   339→ * Get the singleton instance of the Electron backend
   340→ */
   341→export function getElectronBackend(): ElectronBackend {
   342→  if (!instance) {
   343→    instance = new ElectronBackend()
   344→  }
   345→  return instance
   346→}
   347→
   348→/**
   349→ * Check if the Electron API is available
   350→ */
   351→export function isElectronAvailable(): boolean {
   352→  return typeof window !== 'undefined' && !!window.electronAPI
   353→}

1→/**
     2→ * API Abstraction Layer Types
     3→ *
     4→ * This module defines the contract for the API interface that can be implemented
     5→ * by both Electron IPC (desktop) and HTTP/WebSocket (web/mobile) backends.
     6→ */
     7→
     8→// ============================================================================
     9→// Connection State Types
    10→// ============================================================================
    11→
    12→/**
    13→ * Connection state for HTTP backend
    14→ */
    15→export type ConnectionState = 'disconnected' | 'connecting' | 'connected' | 'error'
    16→
    17→/**
    18→ * Type of API backend being used
    19→ */
    20→export type ApiBackendType = 'electron' | 'http'
    21→
    22→// ============================================================================
    23→// Data Types
    24→// ============================================================================
    25→
    26→/**
    27→ * Terminal ANSI colors customization
    28→ */
    29→export interface TerminalColorsCustomization {
    30→  black?: string
    31→  red?: string
    32→  green?: string
    33→  yellow?: string
    34→  blue?: string
    35→  magenta?: string
    36→  cyan?: string
    37→  white?: string
    38→}
    39→
    40→/**
    41→ * Theme customization settings
    42→ */
    43→export interface ThemeCustomization {
    44→  accentColor: string | null
    45→  backgroundColor: string | null
    46→  textColor: string | null
    47→  terminalColors: TerminalColorsCustomization | null
    48→}
    49→
    50→/**
    51→ * Application settings
    52→ */
    53→export interface Settings {
    54→  defaultProjectDir: string
    55→  theme: string

    57→  voiceOutputEnabled?: boolean

    66→

    76→
    77→/**
    78→ * Project configuration

    80→export interface Project {
    81→  path: string

    86→  apiSessionMode?: 'existing' | 'new-keep' | 'new-close'

    97→
    98→/**
    99→ * Open tab representing an active terminal session
   100→ */
   101→export interface OpenTab {
   102→  id: string

   113→export interface TileLayout {

   123→/**

   151→
   152→// ============================================================================
   153→// API Interface
   154→// ============================================================================
   155→
   156→export type Unsubscribe = () => void
   157→
   158→export type PtyDataCallback = (data: string) => void
   159→export type PtyExitCallback = (code: number) => void
   160→export type PtyRecreatedCallback = (data: { oldId: string; newId: string; backend: BackendId }) => void
   161→
   162→export type BackendId = 'claude' | 'gemini' | 'codex' | 'opencode' | 'aider'

   165→export interface ApiOpenSessionEvent {

   173→/**

   176→export interface Api {

   180→  voiceGetSettings?: () => Promise<{ ttsVoice?: string; ttsEngine?: string; ttsSpeed?: number; xttsTemperature?: number; xttsTopK?: number; xttsTopP?: number; xttsRepetitionPenalty?: number }>
   181→  voiceCheckWhisper?: () => Promise<{ installed: boolean; models: string[]; currentModel: string | null }>

   184→  voiceApplySettings?: (settings: { ttsVoice?: string; ttsEngine?: string; ttsSpeed?: number; xttsTemperature?: number; xttsTopK?: number; xttsTopP?: number; xttsRepetitionPenalty?: number }) => Promise<{ success: boolean }>
   185→  voiceSetVoice?: (voice: string | { voice: string; engine: 'piper' | 'xtts' }) => Promise<{ success: boolean }>

   189→  // Optional: API server control (desktop only)

   191→  apiStop?: (projectPath: string) => Promise<{ success: boolean }>
   192→
   193→  // ==========================================================================
   194→  // Workspace Management
   195→  // ==========================================================================
   196→
   197→  /**
   198→   * Get the current workspace state
   199→   * @returns Promise resolving to workspace data
   200→   */
   201→  getWorkspace: () => Promise<Workspace>
   202→
   203→  /**
   204→   * Save the workspace state
   205→   * @param workspace Workspace data to save

   208→
   209→  // ==========================================================================
   210→  // Settings Management
   211→  // ==========================================================================
   212→
   213→  /**
   214→   * Get application settings
   215→   * @returns Promise resolving to settings
   216→   */
   217→  getSettings: () => Promise<Settings>
   218→
   219→  /**

   221→   * @param settings Settings to save
   222→   */
   223→  saveSettings: (settings: Settings) => Promise<void>
   224→
   225→  // ==========================================================================
   226→  // Project Management
   227→  // ==========================================================================
   228→
   229→  /**
   230→   * Open a directory picker to add a project
   231→   * @returns Promise resolving to selected path or null
   232→   */
   233→  addProject: () => Promise<string | null>
   234→
   235→  /**
   236→   * Open a directory picker to select a parent folder and add all subdirectories as projects
   237→   * @returns Promise resolving to array of projects or null
   238→   */
   239→  addProjectsFromParent: () => Promise<Array<{ path: string; name: string }> | null>
   240→
   241→  // ==========================================================================
   242→  // Session Discovery
   243→  // ==========================================================================
   244→
   245→  /**
   246→   * Discover recent sessions for a project
   247→   * @param projectPath Path to the project
   248→   * @param backend Optional backend type ('claude', 'gemini', 'codex', 'opencode', or 'aider')
   249→   */
   250→  discoverSessions: (projectPath: string, backend?: BackendId) => Promise<Session[]>
   251→
   252→  // ==========================================================================
   253→  // PTY Management
   254→  // ==========================================================================
   255→
   256→  /**
   257→   * Spawn a new PTY session
   258→   * @param cwd Working directory

   263→  spawnPty: (cwd: string, sessionId?: string, model?: string, backend?: BackendId) => Promise<string>
   264→
   265→  /**
   266→   * Write data to a PTY
   267→   */
   268→  writePty: (id: string, data: string) => void
   269→
   270→  /**

   274→
   275→  /**

   279→
   280→  /**

   289→

   294→
   295→  /**

   298→  onPtyRecreated: (callback: (data: { oldId: string; newId: string; backend: BackendId }) => void) => Unsubscribe
   299→
   300→  // ==========================================================================

   304→  /**

   324→  // ==========================================================================

   328→  /**

   335→  /**

   342→// ============================================================================

   350→ */
   351→export interface ExtendedApi extends Api {

   367→

   376→}
   377→
   378→// ============================================================================
   379→// API Context Type

1→/**
     2→ * WebSocket Manager - WebSocket handling
     3→ */
     4→
     5→import { WebSocket, WebSocketServer } from 'ws'
     6→import { Server } from 'http'
     7→import { log } from './utils'
     8→import { PendingFile } from './types'
     9→
    10→export interface WebSocketManagerDeps {
    11→  getToken: () => string
    12→  getPtyManager: () => any
    13→  getPort: () => number
    14→  getTerminalSubscriptions: () => Map<string, Set<WebSocket>>
    15→  getPtyStreams: () => Map<string, Set<WebSocket>>
    16→  getPtyDataBuffer: () => Map<string, string[]>
    17→  getConnectedClients: () => Set<WebSocket>
    18→  getPendingFiles: () => Map<string, PendingFile>
    19→}
    20→
    21→export function setupWebSocket(server: Server, deps: WebSocketManagerDeps): WebSocketServer {
    22→  const wss = new WebSocketServer({ noServer: true })
    23→
    24→  // Handle upgrade requests manually to support multiple paths
    25→  server.on('upgrade', (req, socket, head) => {
    26→    const url = new URL(req.url || '', `http://localhost:${deps.getPort()}`)
    27→    const pathname = url.pathname
    28→
    29→    // Get token from Sec-WebSocket-Protocol header (preferred) or query string (fallback)
    30→    const protocolHeader = req.headers['sec-websocket-protocol'] as string | undefined
    31→    const protocols = protocolHeader?.split(',').map(p => p.trim()) || []
    32→    const tokenFromProtocol = protocols.find(p => p.startsWith('token-'))?.slice(6)
    33→    const tokenFromQuery = url.searchParams.get('token')
    34→    const token = tokenFromProtocol || tokenFromQuery
    35→
    36→    log('WebSocket upgrade request', {
    37→      url: req.url,
    38→      pathname,
    39→      hasProtocolToken: !!tokenFromProtocol,
    40→      hasQueryToken: !!tokenFromQuery,
    41→      headers: {
    42→        host: req.headers.host,
    43→        origin: req.headers.origin,
    44→        upgrade: req.headers.upgrade
    45→      }
    46→    })
    47→
    48→    // Validate token for all WebSocket connections
    49→    if (token !== deps.getToken()) {
    50→      log('WebSocket auth failed', { providedToken: token?.slice(0, 8) })
    51→      socket.write('HTTP/1.1 401 Unauthorized\r\n\r\n')
    52→      socket.destroy()
    53→      return
    54→    }
    55→
    56→    // Handle PTY stream WebSocket: /api/pty/:id/stream
    57→    const ptyStreamMatch = pathname.match(/^\/api\/pty\/([^/]+)\/stream$/)
    58→    if (ptyStreamMatch) {
    59→      const ptyId = ptyStreamMatch[1]
    60→      handlePtyStreamUpgrade(req, socket, head, ptyId, deps)
    61→      return
    62→    }
    63→
    64→    // Handle main WebSocket path: /ws
    65→    if (pathname === '/ws') {
    66→      wss.handleUpgrade(req, socket, head, (ws) => {
    67→        wss.emit('connection', ws, req)
    68→      })
    69→      return
    70→    }
    71→
    72→    // Unknown WebSocket path
    73→    log('Unknown WebSocket path', { pathname })
    74→    socket.write('HTTP/1.1 404 Not Found\r\n\r\n')
    75→    socket.destroy()
    76→  })
    77→
    78→  wss.on('connection', (ws: WebSocket, _req) => {
    79→    log('WebSocket client connected (main)')
    80→
    81→    deps.getConnectedClients().add(ws)
    82→
    83→    ws.on('message', (message: Buffer) => {
    84→      try {
    85→        const msg = JSON.parse(message.toString())
    86→        handleWebSocketMessage(ws, msg, deps)
    87→      } catch (e) {
    88→        log('Invalid WebSocket message', { error: String(e) })
    89→      }
    90→    })
    91→
    92→    ws.on('close', () => {
    93→      deps.getConnectedClients().delete(ws)
    94→
    95→      deps.getTerminalSubscriptions().forEach((subscribers, ptyId) => {
    96→        subscribers.delete(ws)
    97→        if (subscribers.size === 0) {
    98→          deps.getTerminalSubscriptions().delete(ptyId)
    99→        }
   100→      })
   101→      log('WebSocket client disconnected (main)')
   102→    })
   103→
   104→    // Send welcome message
   105→    ws.send(JSON.stringify({ type: 'connected', timestamp: Date.now() }))
   106→
   107→    // Send any pending files to new client
   108→    sendPendingFilesToClient(ws, deps.getPendingFiles())
   109→  })
   110→
   111→  return wss
   112→}

   147→        const msg = JSON.parse(message.toString())
   148→
   149→        switch (msg.type) {
   150→          case 'input':
   151→            if (msg.data && ptyManager) {
   152→              ptyManager.write(ptyId, msg.data)
   153→            }
   154→            break
   155→
   156→          case 'resize':
   157→            if (msg.cols && msg.rows && ptyManager) {
   158→              ptyManager.resize(ptyId, msg.cols, msg.rows)
   159→            }
   160→            break
   161→
   162→          case 'ping':
   163→            ws.send(JSON.stringify({ type: 'pong', timestamp: Date.now() }))
   164→            break
   165→
   166→          default:
   167→            log('Unknown PTY stream message type', { type: msg.type, ptyId })
   168→        }
   169→      } catch (e) {
   170→        log('Invalid PTY stream message', { error: String(e), ptyId })
   171→      }
   172→    })
   173→
   174→    ws.on('close', () => {
   175→      log('PTY stream disconnected', { ptyId })
   176→      ptyStreams.get(ptyId)?.delete(ws)
   177→      if (ptyStreams.get(ptyId)?.size === 0) {
   178→        ptyStreams.delete(ptyId)
   179→      }
   180→    })

   182→    ws.on('error', (err) => {
   183→      log('PTY stream error', { error: String(err), ptyId })
   184→      ptyStreams.get(ptyId)?.delete(ws)
   185→    })
   186→  })
   187→}

   189→function handleWebSocketMessage(ws: WebSocket, msg: any, deps: WebSocketManagerDeps): void {

     1→/**
     2→ * WebSocket Manager - WebSocket handling
     3→ */
     4→
     5→import { WebSocket, WebSocketServer } from 'ws'
     6→import { Server } from 'http'
     7→import { log } from './utils'
     8→import { PendingFile } from './types'
     9→
    10→export interface WebSocketManagerDeps {
    11→  getToken: () => string
    12→  getPtyManager: () => any
    13→  getPort: () => number
    14→  getTerminalSubscriptions: () => Map<string, Set<WebSocket>>
    15→  getPtyStreams: () => Map<string, Set<WebSocket>>
    16→  getPtyDataBuffer: () => Map<string, string[]>
    17→  getConnectedClients: () => Set<WebSocket>
    18→  getPendingFiles: () => Map<string, PendingFile>
    19→}
    20→
    21→export function setupWebSocket(server: Server, deps: WebSocketManagerDeps): WebSocketServer {
    22→  const wss = new WebSocketServer({ noServer: true })
    23→
    24→  // Handle upgrade requests manually to support multiple paths
    25→  server.on('upgrade', (req, socket, head) => {
    26→    const url = new URL(req.url || '', `http://localhost:${deps.getPort()}`)
    27→    const pathname = url.pathname
    28→
    29→    // Get token from Sec-WebSocket-Protocol header (preferred) or query string (fallback)
    30→    const protocolHeader = req.headers['sec-websocket-protocol'] as string | undefined
    31→    const protocols = protocolHeader?.split(',').map(p => p.trim()) || []
    32→    const tokenFromProtocol = protocols.find(p => p.startsWith('token-'))?.slice(6)
    33→    const tokenFromQuery = url.searchParams.get('token')
    34→    const token = tokenFromProtocol || tokenFromQuery
    35→
    36→    log('WebSocket upgrade request', {
    37→      url: req.url,
    38→      pathname,
    39→      hasProtocolToken: !!tokenFromProtocol,
    40→      hasQueryToken: !!tokenFromQuery,
    41→      headers: {
    42→        host: req.headers.host,
    43→        origin: req.headers.origin,
    44→        upgrade: req.headers.upgrade
    45→      }
    46→    })
    47→
    48→    // Validate token for all WebSocket connections
    49→    if (token !== deps.getToken()) {
    50→      log('WebSocket auth failed', { providedToken: token?.slice(0, 8) })
    51→      socket.write('HTTP/1.1 401 Unauthorized\r\n\r\n')
    52→      socket.destroy()
    53→      return
    54→    }
    55→
    56→    // Handle PTY stream WebSocket: /api/pty/:id/stream
    57→    const ptyStreamMatch = pathname.match(/^\/api\/pty\/([^/]+)\/stream$/)
    58→    if (ptyStreamMatch) {
    59→      const ptyId = ptyStreamMatch[1]
    60→      handlePtyStreamUpgrade(req, socket, head, ptyId, deps)
    61→      return
    62→    }
    63→
    64→    // Handle main WebSocket path: /ws
    65→    if (pathname === '/ws') {
    66→      wss.handleUpgrade(req, socket, head, (ws) => {
    67→        wss.emit('connection', ws, req)
    68→      })
    69→      return
    70→    }
    71→
    72→    // Unknown WebSocket path
    73→    log('Unknown WebSocket path', { pathname })
    74→    socket.write('HTTP/1.1 404 Not Found\r\n\r\n')
    75→    socket.destroy()
    76→  })
    77→
    78→  wss.on('connection', (ws: WebSocket, _req) => {
    79→    log('WebSocket client connected (main)')
    80→
    81→    deps.getConnectedClients().add(ws)
    82→
    83→    ws.on('message', (message: Buffer) => {
    84→      try {
    85→        const msg = JSON.parse(message.toString())
    86→        handleWebSocketMessage(ws, msg, deps)
    87→      } catch (e) {
    88→        log('Invalid WebSocket message', { error: String(e) })
    89→      }
    90→    })
    91→
    92→    ws.on('close', () => {
    93→      deps.getConnectedClients().delete(ws)
    94→
    95→      deps.getTerminalSubscriptions().forEach((subscribers, ptyId) => {
    96→        subscribers.delete(ws)
    97→        if (subscribers.size === 0) {
    98→          deps.getTerminalSubscriptions().delete(ptyId)
    99→        }
   100→      })
   101→      log('WebSocket client disconnected (main)')
   102→    })
   103→
   104→    // Send welcome message
   105→    ws.send(JSON.stringify({ type: 'connected', timestamp: Date.now() }))
   106→
   107→    // Send any pending files to new client
   108→    sendPendingFilesToClient(ws, deps.getPendingFiles())
   109→  })
   110→
   111→  return wss
   112→}
   113→
   114→function handlePtyStreamUpgrade(
   115→  req: any,
   116→  socket: any,
   117→  head: any,
   118→  ptyId: string,
   119→  deps: WebSocketManagerDeps
   120→): void {
   121→  const ptyManager = deps.getPtyManager()
   122→  if (!ptyManager || !ptyManager.getProcess(ptyId)) {
   123→    log('PTY stream upgrade failed - PTY not found', { ptyId })
   124→    socket.write('HTTP/1.1 404 Not Found\r\n\r\n')
   125→    socket.destroy()
   126→    return
   127→  }
   128→
   129→  const streamWss = new WebSocketServer({ noServer: true })
   130→
   131→  streamWss.handleUpgrade(req, socket, head, (ws) => {
   132→    log('PTY stream connected', { ptyId })
   133→
   134→    const ptyStreams = deps.getPtyStreams()
   135→    if (!ptyStreams.has(ptyId)) {
   136→      ptyStreams.set(ptyId, new Set())
   137→    }
   138→    ptyStreams.get(ptyId)!.add(ws)
   139→
   140→    ws.send(JSON.stringify({ type: 'connected', ptyId }))
   141→
   142→    // Flush any buffered data
   143→    flushPtyBuffer(ptyId, ws, deps.getPtyDataBuffer())
   144→
   145→    ws.on('message', (message: Buffer) => {
   146→      try {
   147→        const msg = JSON.parse(message.toString())
   148→
   149→        switch (msg.type) {
   150→          case 'input':
   151→            if (msg.data && ptyManager) {
   152→              ptyManager.write(ptyId, msg.data)
   153→            }
   154→            break
   155→
   156→          case 'resize':
   157→            if (msg.cols && msg.rows && ptyManager) {
   158→              ptyManager.resize(ptyId, msg.cols, msg.rows)
   159→            }
   160→            break
   161→
   162→          case 'ping':
   163→            ws.send(JSON.stringify({ type: 'pong', timestamp: Date.now() }))
   164→            break
   165→
   166→          default:
   167→            log('Unknown PTY stream message type', { type: msg.type, ptyId })
   168→        }
   169→      } catch (e) {
   170→        log('Invalid PTY stream message', { error: String(e), ptyId })
   171→      }
   172→    })
   173→
   174→    ws.on('close', () => {
   175→      log('PTY stream disconnected', { ptyId })
   176→      ptyStreams.get(ptyId)?.delete(ws)
   177→      if (ptyStreams.get(ptyId)?.size === 0) {
   178→        ptyStreams.delete(ptyId)
   179→      }
   180→    })
   181→
   182→    ws.on('error', (err) => {
   183→      log('PTY stream error', { error: String(err), ptyId })
   184→      ptyStreams.get(ptyId)?.delete(ws)
   185→    })
   186→  })
   187→}
   188→
   189→function handleWebSocketMessage(ws: WebSocket, msg: any, deps: WebSocketManagerDeps): void {
   190→  const ptyManager = deps.getPtyManager()
   191→  const terminalSubscriptions = deps.getTerminalSubscriptions()
   192→
   193→  switch (msg.type) {
   194→    case 'subscribe':
   195→      if (msg.ptyId) {
   196→        if (!terminalSubscriptions.has(msg.ptyId)) {
   197→          terminalSubscriptions.set(msg.ptyId, new Set())
   198→        }
   199→        terminalSubscriptions.get(msg.ptyId)!.add(ws)
   200→        ws.send(JSON.stringify({ type: 'subscribed', ptyId: msg.ptyId }))

    37→import type { PtyOperations, UseTerminalSetupOptions } from './types.js'
    38→
    39→interface InitState {
    40→  disposed: boolean
    41→  webglAddonRef: { current: { dispose: () => void } | null }
    42→  terminal: XTerm | null
    43→  fitAddon: FitAddon | null
    44→  cleanupScroll: { dispose: () => void } | null
    45→  resizeObserver: ResizeObserver | null

    52→
    53→/**
    54→ * Configures mobile keyboard attributes on the terminal's internal textarea.
    55→ */
    56→function configureMobileKeyboard(textarea: HTMLTextAreaElement): void {
    57→  textarea.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'off')
    58→  textarea.setAttribute('autocorrect', 'off')
    59→  textarea.setAttribute('autocapitalize', 'off')
    60→  textarea.setAttribute('spellcheck', 'false')
    61→  textarea.setAttribute('enterkeyhint', 'send')
    62→  textarea.setAttribute('inputmode', 'text')
    63→  textarea.autocomplete = 'off'
    64→  ;(textarea as any).autocorrect = 'off'
    65→  ;(textarea as any).autocapitalize = 'off'
    66→  textarea.spellcheck = false
    67→  console.log('[Terminal] Disabled mobile keyboard features on textarea')
    68→}
    69→
    70→/**
    71→ * Loads the WebGL addon for GPU acceleration.
    72→ */
    73→function loadWebGLAddon(
    74→  terminal: XTerm,
    75→  fitAddon: FitAddon,
    76→  state: InitState
    77→): void {
    78→  if (!ENABLE_WEBGL) return
    79→
    80→  setTimeout(() => {
    81→    if (state.disposed || !terminal) return
    82→
    83→    let dims: { cols: number; rows: number } | undefined
    84→    try {
    85→      dims = fitAddon.proposeDimensions()
    86→    } catch {
    87→      return
    88→    }
    89→    if (!dims || dims.cols <= 0 || dims.rows <= 0) {
    90→      console.warn('Terminal GPU acceleration: skipped (no dimensions)')
    91→      return
    92→    }
    93→
    94→    fitAddon.fit()
    95→    import('@xterm/addon-webgl').then(({ WebglAddon }) => {
    96→      if (state.disposed || !terminal) return
    97→      try {
    98→        const webglAddon = new WebglAddon()
    99→        state.webglAddonRef.current = webglAddon
   100→        webglAddon.onContextLoss(() => {
   101→          state.webglAddonRef.current = null
   102→          webglAddon.dispose()
   103→        })
   104→        terminal.loadAddon(webglAddon)
   105→        console.log('Terminal GPU acceleration: WebGL enabled')
   106→      } catch (e) {
   107→        console.warn('Terminal GPU acceleration: WebGL failed, using canvas:', e)
   108→      }
   109→    }).catch(e => {
   110→      console.warn('Terminal GPU acceleration: WebGL unavailable, using canvas:', e)
   111→    })
   112→  }, 100)
   113→}

   117→ */
   118→function loadWebLinksAddon(terminal: XTerm, state: InitState): void {

   121→    try {

   123→        window.electronAPI?.openExternal?.(uri) ?? window.open(uri, '_blank')
   124→      })

     8→export interface DiscoveredSession {

    16→export type SessionBackend = 'claude' | 'gemini' | 'codex' | 'opencode' | 'aider'

    18→// Message types that indicate actual conversation content (not just summaries)
    19→const CONVERSATION_TYPES = ['user', 'assistant']
    20→const OPENCODE_CONFIG_NAME = '.opencode.json'
    21→
    22→function encodeProjectPath(projectPath: string): string {
    23→  // Claude encodes paths by:
    24→  // 1. Removing trailing slashes/backslashes
    25→  // 2. Replacing / and \ with -
    26→  // 3. Replacing _ with -
    27→  // 4. Replacing spaces with -
    28→  // 5. Replacing : with - (Windows drive letters)
    29→  // 6. Replacing dots with dashes (e.g. .config -> -config)
    30→  // /home/user/my_project/ becomes -home-user-my-project
    31→  // /home/user/.config/app becomes -home-user--config-app
    32→  // C:\Users\bob\project becomes -C-Users-bob-project
    33→  return projectPath
    34→    .replace(/[/\\]+$/, '')  // Remove trailing slashes/backslashes
    35→    .replace(/[/\\]/g, '-')  // Replace / and \ with -
    36→    .replace(/:/g, '-')      // Replace : with - (Windows drive letters)
    37→    .replace(/_/g, '-')      // Replace _ with -
    38→    .replace(/ /g, '-')      // Replace spaces with -
    39→    .replace(/\./g, '-')     // Replace dots with dashes
    40→}
    41→
    42→function expandHomePath(targetPath: string): string {
    43→  if (targetPath === '~') {
    44→    return homedir()
    45→  }
    46→  if (targetPath.startsWith('~/') || targetPath.startsWith('~\\')) {
    47→    return join(homedir(), targetPath.slice(2))
    48→  }
    49→  return targetPath
    50→}
    51→
    52→function loadOpenCodeConfig(projectPath: string): { data?: { directory?: string } } | null {
    53→  const home = homedir()
    54→  const xdgConfig = process.env.XDG_CONFIG_HOME
    55→  const candidatePaths = [
    56→    join(projectPath, OPENCODE_CONFIG_NAME),
    57→    ...(xdgConfig ? [join(xdgConfig, 'opencode', OPENCODE_CONFIG_NAME)] : []),
    58→    join(home, '.config', 'opencode', OPENCODE_CONFIG_NAME),
    59→    join(home, OPENCODE_CONFIG_NAME)
    60→  ]
    61→
    62→  for (const configPath of candidatePaths) {
    63→    if (!existsSync(configPath)) continue
    64→    try {
    65→      const content = readFileSync(configPath, 'utf-8')
    66→      return JSON.parse(content)
    67→    } catch (e) {
    68→      console.error('Failed to parse OpenCode config:', e)
    69→    }
    70→  }
    71→
    72→  return null
    73→}
    74→
    75→function resolveOpenCodeDataDir(projectPath: string): string {
    76→  const config = loadOpenCodeConfig(projectPath)
