    22→function encodeProjectPath(projectPath: string): string {
    23→  // Claude encodes paths by:
    24→  // 1. Removing trailing slashes/backslashes
    25→  // 2. Replacing / and \ with -
    26→  // 3. Replacing _ with -
    27→  // 4. Replacing spaces with -
    28→  // 5. Replacing : with - (Windows drive letters)
    29→  // 6. Replacing dots with dashes (e.g. .config -> -config)
    30→  // /home/user/my_project/ becomes -home-user-my-project
    31→  // /home/user/.config/app becomes -home-user--config-app
    32→  // C:\Users\bob\project becomes -C-Users-bob-project
    33→  return projectPath
    34→    .replace(/[/\\]+$/, '')  // Remove trailing slashes/backslashes
    35→    .replace(/[/\\]/g, '-')  // Replace / and \ with -
    36→    .replace(/:/g, '-')      // Replace : with - (Windows drive letters)
    37→    .replace(/_/g, '-')      // Replace _ with -
    38→    .replace(/ /g, '-')      // Replace spaces with -
    39→    .replace(/\./g, '-')     // Replace dots with dashes
    40→}
    41→
    42→function expandHomePath(targetPath: string): string {
    43→  if (targetPath === '~') {
    44→    return homedir()
    45→  }
    46→  if (targetPath.startsWith('~/') || targetPath.startsWith('~\\')) {
    47→    return join(homedir(), targetPath.slice(2))
    48→  }
    49→  return targetPath
    50→}
    51→
    52→function loadOpenCodeConfig(projectPath: string): { data?: { directory?: string } } | null {
    53→  const home = homedir()
    54→  const xdgConfig = process.env.XDG_CONFIG_HOME
    55→  const candidatePaths = [
    56→    join(projectPath, OPENCODE_CONFIG_NAME),
    57→    ...(xdgConfig ? [join(xdgConfig, 'opencode', OPENCODE_CONFIG_NAME)] : []),
    58→    join(home, '.config', 'opencode', OPENCODE_CONFIG_NAME),
    59→    join(home, OPENCODE_CONFIG_NAME)
    60→  ]
    61→
    62→  for (const configPath of candidatePaths) {
    63→    if (!existsSync(configPath)) continue
    64→    try {
    65→      const content = readFileSync(configPath, 'utf-8')
    66→      return JSON.parse(content)
    67→    } catch (e) {
    68→      console.error('Failed to parse OpenCode config:', e)
    69→    }
    70→  }
    71→
    72→  return null
    73→}
    74→
    75→function resolveOpenCodeDataDir(projectPath: string): string {
    76→  const config = loadOpenCodeConfig(projectPath)
    77→  const configured = config?.data?.directory?.trim()
    78→  const dataDir = configured && configured.length > 0 ? configured : '.opencode'
    79→  const expanded = expandHomePath(dataDir)
    80→  return isAbsolute(expanded) ? expanded : join(projectPath, expanded)
    81→}
    82→
    83→async function discoverClaudeSessions(projectPath: string): Promise<DiscoveredSession[]> {
    84→  const claudeDir = join(homedir(), '.claude', 'projects')
    85→  const encodedPath = encodeProjectPath(projectPath)

    88→  if (!existsSync(projectSessionsDir)) {

    96→
    97→    // Process files in parallel for better performance
    98→    const results = await Promise.all(
    99→      files
   100→        .filter(file => file.endsWith('.jsonl') && !file.startsWith('agent-'))
   101→        .map(async (file) => {
   102→          const sessionId = file.replace('.jsonl', '')
   103→          const filePath = join(projectSessionsDir, file)

   108→            const lines = content.split('\n').filter(line => line.trim())

   110→            // Look for slug and check if session has actual conversation
   111→            let slug = sessionId.slice(0, 8)
   112→            let cwd = projectPath
   113→            let hasConversation = false

   115→            for (const line of lines) {

   119→                if (data.cwd) cwd = data.cwd
   120→                if (data.type && CONVERSATION_TYPES.includes(data.type)) {

   136→              cwd,

    34→async function checkCliInstalled(command: string): Promise<boolean> {
    35→  const cached = cliCache.get(command)

    38→  const installed = await execAsync(`${command} --version`, getExecOptions())

     3→ *
     4→ * Bottom bar for terminals with horizontal scrolling.
     5→ * Works on both desktop and mobile.
     6→ * Contains quick input buttons (mobile) and menu categories.
     7→ */
     8→
     9→import React, { useState, useRef, useEffect, useLayoutEffect, useCallback } from 'react'
    10→import ReactDOM from 'react-dom'
    11→import { getCommandMenuItems } from '../utils/backendCommands'
    12→import { AutoWorkOptions } from './TerminalMenu'
    13→
    14→interface TerminalBarProps {
    15→  ptyId: string
    16→  onCommand: (command: string, options?: AutoWorkOptions) => void
    17→  onInput?: (data: string) => void
    18→  currentBackend: 'default' | 'claude' | 'gemini' | 'codex' | 'opencode' | 'aider'
    19→  onBackendChange: (backend: 'default' | 'claude' | 'gemini' | 'codex' | 'opencode' | 'aider') => void
    20→  isMobile?: boolean
    21→  onOpenFileBrowser?: () => void
    22→  onClearWithRestore?: () => void
    23→  onCompactWithRestore?: () => void
    24→}
    25→
    26→// Key codes for quick input
    27→const CTRL_C = '\x03'
    28→const ARROW_UP = '\x1b[A'
    29→const ARROW_DOWN = '\x1b[B'
    30→const TAB = '\t'
    31→const ESCAPE = '\x1b'
    32→const ENTER = '\r'
    33→
    34→interface MenuItem {
    35→  id: string
    36→  label: string
    37→  disabled?: boolean
    38→  isToggle?: boolean
    39→  toggleKey?: keyof AutoWorkOptions
    40→}
    41→
    42→interface MenuCategory {
    43→  id: string
    44→  label: string
    45→  icon: string
    46→  items: MenuItem[]
    47→}
    48→
    49→const AUTOWORK_OPTIONS_KEY = 'terminal-autowork-options'
    50→
    51→const defaultAutoWorkOptions: AutoWorkOptions = {
    52→  withContext: false,
    53→  askQuestions: false,
    54→  pauseForReview: false,
    55→  finalEvaluation: false,
    56→  gitCommitEachTask: false,
    57→}
    58→
    59→export function TerminalBar({
    60→  ptyId,
    61→  onCommand,
    62→  onInput,
    63→  currentBackend,
    64→  onBackendChange,
    65→  isMobile = false,
    66→  onOpenFileBrowser,
    67→  onClearWithRestore,
    68→  onCompactWithRestore
    69→}: TerminalBarProps): React.ReactElement {
    70→  const [openMenu, setOpenMenu] = useState<string | null>(null)
    71→  const [menuPosition, setMenuPosition] = useState({ top: 0, left: 0, visible: false })
    72→  const barRef = useRef<HTMLDivElement>(null)
    73→  const scrollRef = useRef<HTMLDivElement>(null)
    74→  const dropdownRef = useRef<HTMLDivElement>(null)
    75→  const menuButtonRefs = useRef<Record<string, HTMLButtonElement | null>>({})
    76→
    77→  // Auto work options state
    78→  const [autoWorkOptions, setAutoWorkOptions] = useState<AutoWorkOptions>(() => {
    79→    const stored = localStorage.getItem(AUTOWORK_OPTIONS_KEY)
    80→    if (stored) {
    81→      try {

    90→  // Persist autowork options
    91→  useEffect(() => {
    92→    localStorage.setItem(AUTOWORK_OPTIONS_KEY, JSON.stringify(autoWorkOptions))
    93→  }, [autoWorkOptions])
    94→
    95→  // Convert vertical scroll wheel to horizontal scroll on the bar
    96→  useEffect(() => {
    97→    const el = scrollRef.current
    98→    if (!el) return
    99→    const handleWheel = (e: WheelEvent) => {
   100→      if (e.deltaY !== 0 && el.scrollWidth > el.clientWidth) {
   101→        e.preventDefault()
   102→        el.scrollLeft += e.deltaY
   103→      }
   104→    }
   105→    el.addEventListener('wheel', handleWheel, { passive: false })
   106→    return () => el.removeEventListener('wheel', handleWheel)
   107→  }, [])
   108→
   109→  // Close menu when clicking outside
   110→  useEffect(() => {
   111→    const handleClickOutside = (e: MouseEvent) => {
   112→      const target = e.target as Node
   113→      if (barRef.current && barRef.current.contains(target)) return
   114→      if (dropdownRef.current && dropdownRef.current.contains(target)) return
   115→      setOpenMenu(null)
   116→    }
   117→    const handleEscape = (e: KeyboardEvent) => {
   118→      if (e.key === 'Escape') {
   119→        setOpenMenu(null)
   120→      }
   121→    }
   122→    document.addEventListener('mousedown', handleClickOutside)
   123→    document.addEventListener('keydown', handleEscape)
   124→    return () => {
   125→      document.removeEventListener('mousedown', handleClickOutside)
   126→      document.removeEventListener('keydown', handleEscape)
   127→    }
   128→  }, [])
   129→
   130→  useEffect(() => {
   131→    if (!openMenu) {
   132→      setMenuPosition((prev) => prev.visible ? { ...prev, visible: false } : prev)
   133→    }
   134→  }, [openMenu])
   135→
   136→  const updateMenuPosition = useCallback(() => {
   137→    if (!openMenu || !dropdownRef.current) return
   138→    const button = menuButtonRefs.current[openMenu]
   139→    if (!button) return
   140→
   141→    const padding = 8
   142→    const rect = button.getBoundingClientRect()
   143→    const menuRect = dropdownRef.current.getBoundingClientRect()
   144→    const maxTop = window.innerHeight - menuRect.height - padding
   145→    const openDownTop = rect.bottom + 6
   146→
   147→    let top = rect.top - menuRect.height - 6
   148→    if (top < padding) {
   149→      top = Math.min(openDownTop, maxTop)
   150→    }
   151→
   152→    let left = rect.left
   153→    if (left + menuRect.width > window.innerWidth - padding) {
   154→      left = window.innerWidth - menuRect.width - padding
   155→    }
   156→    if (left < padding) {
   157→      left = padding
   158→    }
   159→
   160→    setMenuPosition((prev) => {
   161→      if (prev.top === top && prev.left === left && prev.visible) return prev
   162→      return { top, left, visible: true }
   163→    })
   164→  }, [openMenu])
   165→
   166→  useLayoutEffect(() => {
   167→    updateMenuPosition()
   168→  }, [updateMenuPosition, openMenu])
   169→
   170→  useEffect(() => {
   171→    if (!openMenu) return
   172→    const handleReposition = () => updateMenuPosition()
   173→    window.addEventListener('resize', handleReposition)
   174→    window.addEventListener('scroll', handleReposition, true)
   175→    return () => {
   176→      window.removeEventListener('resize', handleReposition)
   177→      window.removeEventListener('scroll', handleReposition, true)
   178→    }
   179→  }, [openMenu, updateMenuPosition])
   180→
   181→  const commandItems = getCommandMenuItems(currentBackend)
   182→
   183→  const menuCategories: MenuCategory[] = [
   184→    {
   185→      id: 'commands',
   186→      label: 'Commands',
   187→      icon: '/',
   188→      items: commandItems.filter((item) => !item.id.startsWith('divider')),
   189→    },
   190→    {
   191→      id: 'gsd',
   192→      label: 'GSD',
   193→      icon: '📋',
   194→      items: [
   195→        { id: 'gsd:progress', label: 'Check Progress' },
   196→        { id: 'gsd:execute-phase', label: 'Execute Phase' },
   197→        { id: 'gsd:plan-phase', label: 'Plan Phase' },
   198→        { id: 'gsd:new-project', label: 'New Project' },
   199→        { id: 'gsd:map-codebase', label: 'Map Codebase' },
   200→        { id: 'gsd:create-roadmap', label: 'Create Roadmap' },

     1→/**
     2→ * TerminalBar Component
     3→ *
     4→ * Bottom bar for terminals with horizontal scrolling.
     5→ * Works on both desktop and mobile.
     6→ * Contains quick input buttons (mobile) and menu categories.
     7→ */
     8→
     9→import React, { useState, useRef, useEffect, useLayoutEffect, useCallback } from 'react'
    10→import ReactDOM from 'react-dom'
    11→import { getCommandMenuItems } from '../utils/backendCommands'
    12→import { AutoWorkOptions } from './TerminalMenu'
    13→
    14→interface TerminalBarProps {
    15→  ptyId: string
    16→  onCommand: (command: string, options?: AutoWorkOptions) => void
    17→  onInput?: (data: string) => void
    18→  currentBackend: 'default' | 'claude' | 'gemini' | 'codex' | 'opencode' | 'aider'
    19→  onBackendChange: (backend: 'default' | 'claude' | 'gemini' | 'codex' | 'opencode' | 'aider') => void
    20→  isMobile?: boolean
    21→  onOpenFileBrowser?: () => void
    22→  onClearWithRestore?: () => void
    23→  onCompactWithRestore?: () => void
    24→}
    25→
    26→// Key codes for quick input
    27→const CTRL_C = '\x03'
    28→const ARROW_UP = '\x1b[A'
    29→const ARROW_DOWN = '\x1b[B'
    30→const TAB = '\t'
    31→const ESCAPE = '\x1b'
    32→const ENTER = '\r'
    33→
    34→interface MenuItem {
    35→  id: string
    36→  label: string
    37→  disabled?: boolean
    38→  isToggle?: boolean
    39→  toggleKey?: keyof AutoWorkOptions
    40→}
    41→
    42→interface MenuCategory {
    43→  id: string
    44→  label: string
    45→  icon: string
    46→  items: MenuItem[]
    47→}
    48→
    49→const AUTOWORK_OPTIONS_KEY = 'terminal-autowork-options'
    50→
    51→const defaultAutoWorkOptions: AutoWorkOptions = {
    52→  withContext: false,
    53→  askQuestions: false,
    54→  pauseForReview: false,
    55→  finalEvaluation: false,
    56→  gitCommitEachTask: false,
    57→}
    58→
    59→export function TerminalBar({
    60→  ptyId,
    61→  onCommand,
    62→  onInput,
    63→  currentBackend,
    64→  onBackendChange,
    65→  isMobile = false,
    66→  onOpenFileBrowser,
    67→  onClearWithRestore,
    68→  onCompactWithRestore
    69→}: TerminalBarProps): React.ReactElement {
    70→  const [openMenu, setOpenMenu] = useState<string | null>(null)
    71→  const [menuPosition, setMenuPosition] = useState({ top: 0, left: 0, visible: false })
    72→  const barRef = useRef<HTMLDivElement>(null)
    73→  const scrollRef = useRef<HTMLDivElement>(null)
    74→  const dropdownRef = useRef<HTMLDivElement>(null)
    75→  const menuButtonRefs = useRef<Record<string, HTMLButtonElement | null>>({})
    76→
    77→  // Auto work options state
    78→  const [autoWorkOptions, setAutoWorkOptions] = useState<AutoWorkOptions>(() => {
    79→    const stored = localStorage.getItem(AUTOWORK_OPTIONS_KEY)
    80→    if (stored) {
    81→      try {
    82→        return { ...defaultAutoWorkOptions, ...JSON.parse(stored) }
    83→      } catch {
    84→        return defaultAutoWorkOptions
    85→      }
    86→    }
    87→    return defaultAutoWorkOptions
    88→  })
    89→
    90→  // Persist autowork options
    91→  useEffect(() => {
    92→    localStorage.setItem(AUTOWORK_OPTIONS_KEY, JSON.stringify(autoWorkOptions))
    93→  }, [autoWorkOptions])
    94→
    95→  // Convert vertical scroll wheel to horizontal scroll on the bar
    96→  useEffect(() => {
    97→    const el = scrollRef.current
    98→    if (!el) return
    99→    const handleWheel = (e: WheelEvent) => {
   100→      if (e.deltaY !== 0 && el.scrollWidth > el.clientWidth) {
   101→        e.preventDefault()
   102→        el.scrollLeft += e.deltaY
   103→      }
   104→    }
   105→    el.addEventListener('wheel', handleWheel, { passive: false })
   106→    return () => el.removeEventListener('wheel', handleWheel)
   107→  }, [])
   108→
   109→  // Close menu when clicking outside
   110→  useEffect(() => {
   111→    const handleClickOutside = (e: MouseEvent) => {
   112→      const target = e.target as Node
   113→      if (barRef.current && barRef.current.contains(target)) return
   114→      if (dropdownRef.current && dropdownRef.current.contains(target)) return
   115→      setOpenMenu(null)
   116→    }
   117→    const handleEscape = (e: KeyboardEvent) => {
   118→      if (e.key === 'Escape') {
   119→        setOpenMenu(null)
   120→      }
   121→    }
   122→    document.addEventListener('mousedown', handleClickOutside)
   123→    document.addEventListener('keydown', handleEscape)
   124→    return () => {
   125→      document.removeEventListener('mousedown', handleClickOutside)
   126→      document.removeEventListener('keydown', handleEscape)
   127→    }
   128→  }, [])
   129→
   130→  useEffect(() => {
   131→    if (!openMenu) {
   132→      setMenuPosition((prev) => prev.visible ? { ...prev, visible: false } : prev)
   133→    }
   134→  }, [openMenu])
   135→
   136→  const updateMenuPosition = useCallback(() => {
   137→    if (!openMenu || !dropdownRef.current) return
   138→    const button = menuButtonRefs.current[openMenu]
   139→    if (!button) return
   140→
   141→    const padding = 8
   142→    const rect = button.getBoundingClientRect()
   143→    const menuRect = dropdownRef.current.getBoundingClientRect()
   144→    const maxTop = window.innerHeight - menuRect.height - padding
   145→    const openDownTop = rect.bottom + 6
   146→
   147→    let top = rect.top - menuRect.height - 6
   148→    if (top < padding) {
   149→      top = Math.min(openDownTop, maxTop)
   150→    }
   151→
   152→    let left = rect.left
   153→    if (left + menuRect.width > window.innerWidth - padding) {
   154→      left = window.innerWidth - menuRect.width - padding
   155→    }
   156→    if (left < padding) {
   157→      left = padding
   158→    }
   159→
   160→    setMenuPosition((prev) => {
   161→      if (prev.top === top && prev.left === left && prev.visible) return prev
   162→      return { top, left, visible: true }
   163→    })
   164→  }, [openMenu])
   165→
   166→  useLayoutEffect(() => {
   167→    updateMenuPosition()
   168→  }, [updateMenuPosition, openMenu])
   169→
   170→  useEffect(() => {
   171→    if (!openMenu) return
   172→    const handleReposition = () => updateMenuPosition()
   173→    window.addEventListener('resize', handleReposition)
   174→    window.addEventListener('scroll', handleReposition, true)
   175→    return () => {
   176→      window.removeEventListener('resize', handleReposition)
   177→      window.removeEventListener('scroll', handleReposition, true)
   178→    }
   179→  }, [openMenu, updateMenuPosition])
   180→
   181→  const commandItems = getCommandMenuItems(currentBackend)
   182→
   183→  const menuCategories: MenuCategory[] = [
   184→    {
   185→      id: 'commands',
   186→      label: 'Commands',
   187→      icon: '/',
   188→      items: commandItems.filter((item) => !item.id.startsWith('divider')),
   189→    },
   190→    {
   191→      id: 'gsd',
   192→      label: 'GSD',
   193→      icon: '📋',
   194→      items: [
   195→        { id: 'gsd:progress', label: 'Check Progress' },
   196→        { id: 'gsd:execute-phase', label: 'Execute Phase' },

   141→  const [showFileBrowser, setShowFileBrowser] = useState(false)
   142→  const [fileBrowserPath, setFileBrowserPath] = useState<string | null>(null)
   143→  const hadProjectsRef = useRef(false)
   144→  const terminalContainerRef = useRef<HTMLDivElement>(null)
   145→
   146→  // Keep ref in sync for callbacks
   147→  useEffect(() => {
   148→    voiceOutputEnabledRef.current = voiceOutputEnabled
   149→  }, [voiceOutputEnabled])
   150→
   151→  // Apply per-project voice settings when active tab changes
   152→  useEffect(() => {
   153→    if (!activeTabId) {
   154→      setProjectVoice(null)
   155→      return
   156→    }
   157→    const activeTab = openTabs.find(t => t.id === activeTabId)
   158→    if (!activeTab) {
   159→      setProjectVoice(null)
   160→      return
   161→    }
   162→    const project = projects.find(p => p.path === activeTab.projectPath)
   163→    if (project?.ttsVoice && project?.ttsEngine) {
   164→      setProjectVoice({ ttsVoice: project.ttsVoice, ttsEngine: project.ttsEngine })
   165→    } else {
   166→      setProjectVoice(null)
   167→    }
   168→  }, [activeTabId, openTabs, projects, setProjectVoice])
   169→
   170→  // Save workspace when it changes
   171→  useEffect(() => {
   172→    if (!loading) {
   173→      const hadProjects = sessionStorage.getItem('hadProjects') === 'true' || hadProjectsRef.current
   174→
   175→      if (projects.length === 0 && hadProjects) {
   176→        console.warn('Skipping save: projects empty but previously had projects (likely hot reload)')
   177→        return
   178→      }
   179→
   180→      if (projects.length > 0) {
   181→        hadProjectsRef.current = true
   182→        sessionStorage.setItem('hadProjects', 'true')
   183→      }
   184→
   185→      api.saveWorkspace({
   186→        projects,
   187→        openTabs: openTabs.map(t => ({

   189→          projectPath: t.projectPath,
   190→          sessionId: t.sessionId,
   191→          title: t.title,
   192→          ptyId: t.ptyId,
   193→          backend: t.backend
   194→        })),
   195→        activeTabId,
   196→        viewMode,
   197→        tileLayout,
   198→        categories
   199→      })
   200→    }

1→import { useEffect } from 'react'
     2→import type { Api } from '../api'
     3→import type { BackendId } from '../api/types'
     4→import type { AppSettings } from './useSettings'
     5→import { useWorkspaceStore, OpenTab, Project } from '../stores/workspace'
     6→
     7→interface UseApiListenersOptions {
     8→  api: Api
     9→  projects: Project[]
    10→  settings: AppSettings | null
    11→  addTab: (tab: OpenTab) => void
    12→  updateTab: (id: string, updates: Partial<OpenTab>) => void
    13→  setActiveTab: (id: string) => void
    14→}
    15→
    16→export function useApiListeners({
    17→  api,
    18→  projects,
    19→  settings,
    20→  addTab,
    21→  updateTab,
    22→  setActiveTab
    23→}: UseApiListenersOptions): void {
    24→  // Listen for API requests to open new sessions
    25→  useEffect(() => {
    26→    const unsubscribe = api.onApiOpenSession(async ({ projectPath, autoClose, model }: { projectPath: string; autoClose?: boolean; model?: string }) => {
    27→      // Open a new session for this project (API-triggered)
    28→      const modelLabel = model && model !== 'default' ? ` [${model}]` : ''
    29→      const title = `${projectPath.split(/[/\\]/).pop() || projectPath} - API${modelLabel}${autoClose ? ' (auto-close)' : ''}`
    30→
    31→      // Get project and determine effective backend
    32→      const project = projects.find((p) => p.path === projectPath)
    33→
    34→      const effectiveBackend = (project?.backend && project.backend !== 'default'
    35→        ? project.backend
    36→        : (settings?.backend && settings.backend !== 'default'
    37→          ? settings.backend
    38→          : 'claude')) as BackendId
    39→
    40→      try {
    41→        // Always install TTS instructions so Claude uses <tts> tags
    42→        await api.ttsInstallInstructions?.(projectPath)
    43→
    44→        const ptyId = await api.spawnPty(projectPath, undefined, model, effectiveBackend)
    45→        addTab({
    46→          id: ptyId,
    47→          projectPath,
    48→          title,
    49→          ptyId,
    50→          backend: effectiveBackend
    51→        })
    52→      } catch (e: any) {
    53→        console.error('Failed to spawn PTY for API request:', e)
    54→      }
    55→    })
    56→
    57→    return unsubscribe
    58→  }, [api, addTab, projects, settings?.backend])
    59→
    60→  // Listen for PTY recreation events
    61→  useEffect(() => {
    62→    const unsubscribe = api.onPtyRecreated(({ oldId, newId, backend }) => {
    63→      console.log(`PTY recreated: ${oldId} -> ${newId} with backend ${backend}`)
    64→      // Find the tab with the old ID
    65→      const tab = useWorkspaceStore.getState().openTabs.find((t) => t.id === oldId)
    66→      if (tab) {
    67→        // Update the tab with the new ID and backend
    68→        updateTab(oldId, { id: newId, ptyId: newId, backend })
    69→        // If it was the active tab, update the active tab ID
    70→        if (useWorkspaceStore.getState().activeTabId === oldId) {
    71→          setActiveTab(newId)
    72→        }
    73→      }
    74→    })
    75→    return unsubscribe
    76→  }, [api, updateTab, setActiveTab])
    77→}
    78→

     1→import { useEffect } from 'react'
     2→import type { Api } from '../api'
     3→import type { BackendId } from '../api/types'
     4→import type { AppSettings } from './useSettings'
     5→import { useWorkspaceStore, OpenTab, Project } from '../stores/workspace'
     6→
     7→interface UseApiListenersOptions {
     8→  api: Api
     9→  projects: Project[]
    10→  settings: AppSettings | null
    11→  addTab: (tab: OpenTab) => void
    12→  updateTab: (id: string, updates: Partial<OpenTab>) => void
    13→  setActiveTab: (id: string) => void
    14→}
    15→
    16→export function useApiListeners({
    17→  api,
    18→  projects,
    19→  settings,
    20→  addTab,
    21→  updateTab,
    22→  setActiveTab
    23→}: UseApiListenersOptions): void {
    24→  // Listen for API requests to open new sessions
    25→  useEffect(() => {
    26→    const unsubscribe = api.onApiOpenSession(async ({ projectPath, autoClose, model }: { projectPath: string; autoClose?: boolean; model?: string }) => {
    27→      // Open a new session for this project (API-triggered)
    28→      const modelLabel = model && model !== 'default' ? ` [${model}]` : ''
    29→      const title = `${projectPath.split(/[/\\]/).pop() || projectPath} - API${modelLabel}${autoClose ? ' (auto-close)' : ''}`
    30→
    31→      // Get project and determine effective backend
    32→      const project = projects.find((p) => p.path === projectPath)
    33→
    34→      const effectiveBackend = (project?.backend && project.backend !== 'default'
    35→        ? project.backend
    36→        : (settings?.backend && settings.backend !== 'default'
    37→          ? settings.backend
    38→          : 'claude')) as BackendId
    39→
    40→      try {
    41→        // Always install TTS instructions so Claude uses <tts> tags
    42→        await api.ttsInstallInstructions?.(projectPath)
    43→
    44→        const ptyId = await api.spawnPty(projectPath, undefined, model, effectiveBackend)
    45→        addTab({
    46→          id: ptyId,
    47→          projectPath,
    48→          title,
    49→          ptyId,
    50→          backend: effectiveBackend
    51→        })
    52→      } catch (e: any) {
    53→        console.error('Failed to spawn PTY for API request:', e)
    54→      }
    55→    })
    56→
    57→    return unsubscribe
    58→  }, [api, addTab, projects, settings?.backend])
    59→
    60→  // Listen for PTY recreation events
    61→  useEffect(() => {
    62→    const unsubscribe = api.onPtyRecreated(({ oldId, newId, backend }) => {
    63→      console.log(`PTY recreated: ${oldId} -> ${newId} with backend ${backend}`)
    64→      // Find the tab with the old ID
    65→      const tab = useWorkspaceStore.getState().openTabs.find((t) => t.id === oldId)
    66→      if (tab) {
    67→        // Update the tab with the new ID and backend
    68→        updateTab(oldId, { id: newId, ptyId: newId, backend })
    69→        // If it was the active tab, update the active tab ID
    70→        if (useWorkspaceStore.getState().activeTabId === oldId) {
    71→          setActiveTab(newId)
    72→        }
    73→      }
    74→    })
    75→    return unsubscribe
    76→  }, [api, updateTab, setActiveTab])
    77→}
    78→

    47→}
    48→
    49→let mainWindow: BrowserWindow | null = null
    50→const ptyManager = new PtyManager()
    51→const sessionStore = new SessionStore()
    52→const apiServerManager = new ApiServerManager()
    53→const mobileServer = new MobileServer()
    54→
    55→// PTY tracking

    58→
    59→const orchestratorApi = new OrchestratorApi(ptyManager, ptyToProject)
    60→
    61→const getMainWindow = (): BrowserWindow | null => mainWindow
    62→const setMainWindow = (win: BrowserWindow | null): void => { mainWindow = win }
    63→
    64→// Register IPC handlers
    65→registerCliHandlers(getMainWindow)
    66→registerBeadsHandlers(getMainWindow)
    67→registerVoiceHandlers(getMainWindow)
    68→registerExtensionHandlers()
    69→registerWindowHandlers(getMainWindow)
    70→registerGsdHandlers()
    71→registerKspecHandlers()
    72→registerWorkspaceHandlers(sessionStore, getMainWindow)
    73→registerPtyHandlers(ptyManager, sessionStore, apiServerManager, ptyToProject, ptyToBackend, getMainWindow)
    74→registerServerHandlers(apiServerManager, mobileServer)
    75→registerSettingsHandlers(sessionStore, getMainWindow)
    76→
    77→// Setup API prompt handler
    78→setupApiPromptHandler(apiServerManager, sessionStore, ptyManager, ptyToProject, getMainWindow)
    79→
    80→app.whenReady().then(() => {
    81→  // Migrate data from old app name
    82→  const oldConfigDir = join(app.getPath('appData'), 'simple-claude-gui', 'config')
    83→  const newConfigDir = join(app.getPath('userData'), 'config')
    84→  const oldWorkspace = join(oldConfigDir, 'workspace.json')
    85→  const newWorkspace = join(newConfigDir, 'workspace.json')
    86→
    87→  if (existsSync(oldWorkspace)) {
    88→    try {
    89→      const oldSize = statSync(oldWorkspace).size
    90→      let shouldMigrate = false
    91→
    92→      if (!existsSync(newWorkspace)) {
    93→        shouldMigrate = true
    94→      } else {
    95→        const newSize = statSync(newWorkspace).size
    96→        if (oldSize > 500 && newSize < 500) shouldMigrate = true
    97→      }
    98→
    99→      if (shouldMigrate) {
   100→        console.log('Migrating workspace from simple-claude-gui to simple-code-gui...')
   101→        mkdirSync(newConfigDir, { recursive: true })
   102→        copyFileSync(oldWorkspace, newWorkspace)
   103→
   104→        const oldVoice = join(app.getPath('appData'), 'simple-claude-gui', 'voice-settings.json')
   105→        const newVoice = join(app.getPath('userData'), 'voice-settings.json')

   108→        }
   109→        console.log('Migration complete')
   110→      }
   111→    } catch (err) {
   112→      console.error('Workspace migration failed, continuing with empty workspace:', err)
   113→    }
   114→  }
   115→
   116→  // Initialize portable deps PATH

   123→  createApplicationMenu(mainWindow)

   125→  createApplicationMenu(mainWindow)

   127→
   128→  // Start orchestrator API for MCP-based session control
   129→  orchestratorApi.start()
   130→
   131→  // Start mobile server for phone app connectivity
   132→  mobileServer.setPtyManager(ptyManager)
   133→  mobileServer.setSessionStore(sessionStore)
   134→  mobileServer.setVoiceManager(voiceManager)
   135→  mobileServer.start().then(() => {

   138→  }).catch(err => {
   139→    console.error('[Mobile] Failed to start server:', err)

   141→
   142→  app.on('activate', () => {

   145→      createApplicationMenu(mainWindow)

   147→  })
   148→})

   150→app.on('window-all-closed', () => {
   151→  ptyManager.killAll()

   153→})
   154→
   155→app.on('before-quit', () => {
   156→  orchestratorApi.stop()
   157→  mobileServer.stop()
   158→  apiServerManager.stopAll()
   159→  ptyManager.killAll()

Specifically look for:
1. PTY spawn/creation code (node-pty usage)

content.text:
Excellent! Now I have enough information to create a comprehensive report. Let me generate the final summary:

## Summary: PTY Session Management, Backend Switching, and Terminal Session Lifecycle

Based on my thorough exploration of the codebase, here's a complete inventory of all PTY-related files and potential issues:

### **1. PTY SPAWN & CREATION (node-pty)**

#### Primary File:
- **`/home/user/Programs/Claude Projects/Claude-Terminal/src/main/pty-manager.ts`** (621 lines)
  - `class PtyManager` - Core PTY lifecycle manager
  - **Key method**: `spawn()` (lines 414-498)
    - Creates PTY using `pty.spawn()` from node-pty
    - Generates UUID for PTY ID
    - Tracks backend type and session ID
    - Registers onData and onExit listeners
    - Returns PTY ID to caller
  - **Key classes**:
    - `OutputBuffer` (lines 17-47) - Ring buffer for recent PTY output (max 200 lines)
    - `ClaudeProcess` interface (lines 49-61) - Tracks PTY metadata

#### IPC Handler:
- **`/home/user/Programs/Claude Projects/Claude-Terminal/src/main/app/ipc-handlers/pty.ts`** (137 lines)
  - `registerPtyHandlers()` - Registers IPC endpoint `pty:spawn`
  - Resolves effective backend from global/project settings
  - Invokes `ptyManager.spawn()`
  - Sets up data/exit callbacks that send IPC events back to renderer

#### Preload Bridge:
- **`/home/user/Programs/Claude Projects/Claude-Terminal/src/preload/handlers/pty.ts`** (36 lines)
  - `ptyHandlers` - Exposes: `spawnPty()`, `writePty()`, `resizePty()`, `killPty()`, `onPtyData()`, `onPtyExit()`, `onPtyRecreated()`

---

### **2. PTY RESIZE HANDLING**

#### In pty-manager.ts (lines 507-537):
- **Method**: `resize(id, cols, rows)`
- **Debouncing logic** (critical bug area):
  - Skips if dimensions unchanged
  - **Ink backend special case** (gemini): Uses 1500ms debounce for first 5s after spawn (to prevent SIGWINCH-induced re-render loops)
  - Other backends: 50ms debounce
  - Clears pending resizes before setting new timeout
- **Error handling**: Catches resize errors (PTY may have exited already)

#### Mobile/HTTP routing:
- **`/home/user/Programs/Claude Projects/Claude-Terminal/src/main/mobile-server/routes/pty.ts`** (line 111-140)
  - POST `/api/pty/:id/resize` - Validates cols/rows (1-500 range), calls `ptyManager.resize()`

#### WebSocket streaming:
- **`/home/user/Programs/Claude Projects/Claude-Terminal/src/renderer/api/http-backend/pty-websocket.ts`** (line 76)
  - Sends `{type: 'resize', cols, rows}` over WebSocket or falls back to HTTP

---

### **3. OUTPUT BUFFER / RING BUFFER IMPLEMENTATION**

#### Location: pty-manager.ts (lines 14-47)

**Class**: `OutputBuffer`
- **Data structure**: Array of strings (ANSI-stripped lines) + partial line accumulator
- **Max capacity**: 200 lines (OUTPUT_BUFFER_MAX_LINES constant)
- **Methods**:
  - `append(data)` - Splits by newlines, strips ANSI codes (2 regex patterns), maintains partial line
  - `getRecent(maxLines)` - Returns last N lines (default 50)
  - `clear()` - Resets buffer
- **Integration**: Every PTY has OutputBuffer instance; onData events feed it
- **Usage**: Orchestrator API and mobile routes use `readOutput()` to retrieve recent output

#### Potential issue:
- **Memory accumulation**: If many PTYs spawned, buffers persist in memory. Only cleaned on `cleanupProcess()` which fires on exit.

---

### **4. BACKEND SWITCHING LOGIC**

#### Main handler:
- **`/home/user/Programs/Claude Projects/Claude-Terminal/src/main/app/ipc-handlers/pty.ts`** (lines 103-135)
  - **IPC endpoint**: `pty:set-backend` (ipcMain.handle)
  - **Flow**:
    1. Gets old PTY's process metadata (cwd, sessionId, backend)
    2. Kills old PTY
    3. Spawns new PTY with **same cwd**, **same sessionId if backend unchanged**, **new backend**
    4. Re-registers data/exit handlers
    5. Sends `pty:recreated` IPC event with oldId, newId, backend

#### Renderer handling:
- **`/home/user/Programs/Claude Projects/Claude-Terminal/src/renderer/hooks/useApiListeners.ts`** (lines 61-76)
  - Listens for `api.onPtyRecreated()` event
  - Updates tab: changes `tab.id` and `tab.ptyId` to newId
  - Maintains all other tab state (projectPath, sessionId, title)
  - Sets new ID as active if it was active before

#### Terminal component:
- **`/home/user/Programs/Claude Projects/Claude-Terminal/src/renderer/components/terminal/Terminal.tsx`** (lines 35-39)
  - `handleBackendChange()` callback
  - Calls `api.setPtyBackend()` or `window.electronAPI.setPtyBackend()`

#### Potential bugs:
- **Race condition**: If backend switch is called while PTY is exiting, the old exit handler may fire after new PTY is spawned, causing confusion
- **No validation**: No check that backend is actually available/installed before switching
- **Session ID loss**: When switching backends, session ID is discarded unless backend matches (line 108 in pty.ts)
- **Missing error handling**: No error callback if kill/spawn fails during backend switch

---

### **5. SESSION ID MANAGEMENT**

#### Storage:
- **`/home/user/Programs/Claude Projects/Claude-Terminal/src/main/session-store.ts`** (lines 31-38)
  - `OpenTab` interface stores optional `sessionId` field
  - Persisted in workspace.json

#### Discovery:
- **`/home/user/Programs/Claude Projects/Claude-Terminal/src/main/session-discovery.ts`**
  - Discovers past sessions from backend-specific storage directories
  - **Claude**: `~/.claude/projects/{encoded-path}/`
  - **Gemini**: (not shown in code, likely similar)
  - **OpenCode**: Reads `.opencode.json` config for session data dir
  - Filters sessions by conversation presence

#### Passing to PTY:
- **pty-manager.ts** (line 425):
  - `buildResumeArgs()` converts sessionId to backend-specific flags:
    - Claude: `-r {sessionId}`
    - Gemini: `--resume {sessionId}`
    - Codex: `--resume {sessionId}`
    - OpenCode: `--session {sessionId}`
    - Aider: `--restore {sessionId}`

---

### **6. PTY CLEANUP/TERMINATION**

#### Code location: pty-manager.ts (lines 539-577)

**Method**: `cleanupProcess(id)` (private, lines 539-560)
- Clears resize debounce timeout
- Disposes all event listeners
- Removes from tracking maps (processes, dataCallbacks, exitCallbacks)
- Called automatically on PTY exit

**Method**: `kill(id)` (lines 562-577)
- Sends SIGKILL on Unix, default signal on Windows
- Calls `cleanupProcess()` immediately
- Wraps in try-catch to handle already-dead processes

**Method**: `killAll()` (lines 579-585)
- Called on app shutdown and window close
- Iterates through all tracked PTYs and kills them

#### Potential issues:
- **Resource leak if cleanup fails**: try-catch in dispose() swallows errors (line 550-553), but if all disposables throw, listeners may not be fully cleaned
- **No PTY process group cleanup**: Killing parent PTY doesn't guarantee child processes are killed (handled by node-pty, but not explicitly verified)
- **WebSocket cleanup timing**: Mobile server cleanup of ptyStreams/ptyDataBuffer happens on exit event, but race condition if client disconnect happens first

---

### **7. PATH CONSTRUCTION FOR PTY PROCESSES**

#### Location: `/home/user/Programs/Claude Projects/Claude-Terminal/src/main/pty-manager.ts`

**Function**: `getEnhancedEnv()` (lines 68-107)
- Builds enhanced PATH with portable npm/pip dirs + additional paths
- Calls `getEnhancedPathWithPortable()` from platform.ts

#### Backend-specific executable location:
- **For each backend** (claude, gemini, codex, opencode, aider):
  - Windows: Checks for .cmd/.exe in portable npm-global, then system npm paths, falls back to bare command
  - Unix: Returns bare command (relies on PATH)
  - Special case: Aider searches for aider.exe in Python Scripts directory

#### Location checks:
- **`/home/user/Programs/Claude Projects/Claude-Terminal/src/main/platform.ts`** (lines 45-61)
  - `getAdditionalPaths()` - Returns platform-specific search paths
  - `getEnhancedPath()` - Constructs enhanced PATH
  - `getEnhancedPathWithPortable()` - Includes portable bins at front

#### Potential issues:
- **Silent fallback to bare command**: If no executable found in searched paths, just returns `'claude'` etc. and lets PATH resolve it (line 251-252). May fail cryptically if PATH doesn't have it.
- **Windows Path case-sensitivity**: Sets both `PATH` and `Path` to handle Node.js quirks (lines 76-77), but may cause issues if custom PATH vars are set
- **Git Bash assumption**: Windows assumes git-bash must be in PATH (lines 79-100), but doesn't fail gracefully if missing

---

### **8. ADDITIONAL CRITICAL FILES**

#### Mobile server PTY routing:
- **`/home/user/Programs/Claude Projects/Claude-Terminal/src/main/mobile-server/index.ts`** (lines 66-67)
  - Tracks `ptyStreams: Map<string, Set<WebSocket>>` and `ptyDataBuffer: Map<string, string[]>`
  - PTYs spawned via HTTP route broadcast data to all connected WebSocket streams

- **`/home/user/Programs/Claude Projects/Claude-Terminal/src/main/mobile-server/websocket-manager.ts`** (lines 56-187)
  - Handles `/api/pty/{id}/stream` WebSocket upgrade
  - Routes input/resize messages to ptyManager
  - Broadcasts data to all connected clients for that PTY
  - Buffering up to 1000 messages per PTY if no callbacks registered

#### HTTP API layer:
- **`/home/user/Programs/Claude Projects/Claude-Terminal/src/renderer/api/http-backend/pty-api.ts`** (lines 28-89)
  - Client-side PTY API methods
  - Spawns PTY via HTTP, connects WebSocket for data streaming
  - Write/resize operations prefer WebSocket, fall back to HTTP

- **`/home/user/Programs/Claude Projects/Claude-Terminal/src/renderer/api/http-backend/pty-websocket.ts`** (lines 10-188)
  - Manages per-PTY WebSocket state
  - Data buffering (max 1000 messages per PTY)
  - Reconnection logic with exponential backoff (up to 5 attempts)
  - Handles 'data', 'exit', 'connected', 'pong' message types

---

### **KEY BUGS & ISSUES FOUND**

#### 🔴 **CRITICAL**

1. **Backend switch race condition** (pty.ts:103-135)
   - If PTY exit fires between kill and spawn, old PTY's data might be sent to new PTY ID
   - No transactional guarantee that oldId cleanup is complete before new handlers registered

2. **Session ID discarded on backend switch** (pty.ts:108)
   - If switching backends, session ID is set to undefined unless backend matches
   - This breaks session continuity for all backend changes
   - Should preserve sessionId for all backends

3. **No validation of backend availability before switch**
   - Code doesn't check if requested backend is installed
   - Silently falls back to bare command in PATH (findExecutable functions)
   - May fail cryptically if backend not found

#### 🟡 **HIGH**

4. **Memory leak in OutputBuffer on app shutdown**
   - Buffers persist in memory until PTY exits
   - If app force-quit without waiting for PTY exit, OutputBuffer memory is lost (not a leak, but inefficient)
   - Mitigation: `killAll()` calls cleanupProcess immediately, so should be OK

5. **WebSocket data buffer uncapped for HTTP backend** (pty-websocket.ts:70)
   - Limits per-PTY buffer to 1000 messages
   - But if renderer doesn't register data callbacks, messages accumulate
   - Could leak memory with many PTYs and slow/disconnected clients

6. **Resize timeout not cleared on PTY death** (pty-manager.ts:543-545)
   - `cleanupProcess()` clears resize timeout
   - But if resize timeout fires after kill, it calls `proc.pty.resize()` on dead PTY
   - Protected by try-catch in resize (line 530-535), but logged as noise

#### 🟠 **MEDIUM**

7. **No error propagation from backend switch**
   - `pty:set-backend` IPC handler doesn't send error back to renderer if kill/spawn fails
   - Renderer shows no error feedback to user

8. **Incomplete error handling in terminal setup** (terminalInit.ts)
   - Terminal initialization polling runs forever if container never appears
   - 50ms polling interval (line 137) never stops if container becomes null
   - Should add max retry limit or cleanup on disposal

9. **Missing PTY validation after spawn**
   - `spawn()` doesn't verify PTY is actually running before returning ID
   - If spawn fails but doesn't throw, invalid ID is returned

10. **Windows ConPTY not validated**
    - `useConpty: true` is set unconditionally on Windows (line 454)
    - No fallback if ConPTY unavailable on old Windows versions

#### 🔵 **LOW**

11. **Session discovery inefficiency** (session-discovery.ts)
    - Scans all files in session dir on every discovery call
    - No caching, could be slow for projects with many sessions
    - Parallel Promise.all() helps but still O(n)

12. **Portable deps PATH order** (platform.ts:60)
    - Portable bin dirs prepended to PATH
    - Could shadow system bins unintentionally if names collide

---

### **FILE INVENTORY (Complete List)**

| Category | File Path |
|----------|-----------|
| **PTY Core** | `/src/main/pty-manager.ts` |
| **IPC Handlers** | `/src/main/app/ipc-handlers/pty.ts` |
| **Preload Bridge** | `/src/preload/handlers/pty.ts` |
| **Session Store** | `/src/main/session-store.ts` |
| **Session Discovery** | `/src/main/session-discovery.ts` |
| **Platform Abstraction** | `/src/main/platform.ts` |
| **Portable Deps** | `/src/main/portable-deps.ts` |
| **Mobile Server Routes** | `/src/main/mobile-server/routes/pty.ts` |
| **Mobile WebSocket** | `/src/main/mobile-server/websocket-manager.ts` |
| **Mobile Server Main** | `/src/main/mobile-server/index.ts` |
| **HTTP Backend PTY API** | `/src/renderer/api/http-backend/pty-api.ts` |
| **HTTP PTY WebSocket** | `/src/renderer/api/http-backend/pty-websocket.ts` |
| **Electron Backend** | `/src/renderer/api/electron-backend.ts` |
| **API Types** | `/src/renderer/api/types.ts` |
| **Terminal Component** | `/src/renderer/components/terminal/Terminal.tsx` |
| **Terminal Setup Hook** | `/src/renderer/components/terminal/useTerminalSetup/useTerminalSetup.ts` |
| **Terminal Init** | `/src/renderer/components/terminal/useTerminalSetup/terminalInit.ts` |
| **API Listeners Hook** | `/src/renderer/hooks/useApiListeners.ts` |
| **Terminal Bar** | `/src/renderer/components/TerminalBar.tsx` |
| **Main App** | `/src/main/index.ts` |
| **App Setup** | `/src/main/app/app-setup.ts` |

---

«tts»This is a comprehensive inventory of all PTY-related code in the Claude Terminal codebase, including key functions, data structures, and several potential bugs ranging from critical race conditions to medium-priority error handling issues.«/tts»

// Strip all CSI sequences (including private modes like ?25h, ?1049h),
      // OSC sequences, and single-character escape sequences (e.g. \x1b(B)
      const clean = line
        .replace(/\x1b\[[\x20-\x3f]*[\x40-\x7e]/g, '')  // CSI: covers standard + private mode sequences
        .replace(/\x1b\][^\x07\x1b]*(?:\x07|\x1b\\)/g, '')  // OSC: terminated by BEL or ST
        .replace(/\x1b[()][A-Z0-9]/g, '')  // Character set selection (e.g. \x1b(B)
        .trim()

entries.content: Fix backend switch: add error handling and project settings lookup
Fix ANSI stripping regex in OutputBuffer (misses private mode sequences)
Fix backend switch: transfer autoCloseSessions to new PTY ID
Add spawn error handling in pty:set-backend
Build and verify changes compile
Commit, add task note, and submit

rawInput.command:
cd "/home/user/Programs/Claude Projects/Claude-Terminal" && kspec task note @bug-pty "Execution context:
- Background: PTY lifecycle has several bugs in backend switching and output buffer handling
- Scope: Fix backend switch (error handling, settings inheritance, autoClose transfer), fix ANSI regex
- Files: src/main/app/ipc-handlers/pty.ts, src/main/pty-manager.ts
- Verification: TypeScript compiles clean, npm run build succeeds

Bugs fixed:
1. pty:set-backend lost project settings (autoAcceptTools, permissionMode) — now looks up workspace/project settings
2. pty:set-backend had no error handling — spawn failure after kill left user with no PTY; now throws to renderer
3. pty:set-backend didn't transfer autoCloseSessions — flag leaked for old ID; now transferred
4. pty:set-backend onData/onExit lacked try-catch on IPC send — inconsistent with pty:spawn handler
5. OutputBuffer ANSI regex missed private mode sequences (\x1b[?25h etc.), character set escapes (\x1b(B), and OSC with ST terminator — all now stripped"

data.title:
cd "/home/user/Programs/Claude Projects/Claude-Terminal" && kspec task note @bug-pty "Execution context:
- Background: PTY lifecycle has several bugs in backend switching and output buffer handling
- Scope: Fix backend switch (error handling, settings inheritance, autoClose transfer), fix ANSI regex
- Files: src/main/app/ipc-handlers/pty.ts, src/main/pty-manager.ts
- Verification: TypeScript compiles clean, npm run build succeeds

Bugs fixed:
1. pty:set-backend lost project settings (autoAcceptTools, permissionMode) — now looks up workspace/project settings
2. pty:set-backend had no error handling — spawn failure after kill left user with no PTY; now throws to renderer
3. pty:set-backend didn't transfer autoCloseSessions — flag leaked for old ID; now transferred
4. pty:set-backend onData/onExit lacked try-catch on IPC send — inconsistent with pty:spawn handler
5. OutputBuffer ANSI regex missed private mode sequences (\x1b[?25h etc.), character set escapes (\x1b(B), and OSC with ST terminator — all now stripped"
